attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0 wires
     scope into sync, push, and finalize.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]
- Push hardening: cycle close-out, 0.39.0-2 skipped (0.39.0) [67]
- Scope generalization: init --repo-local + --repo-remote (0.40.0-1) [70]
- Scope generalization: init --scope=code|bot|code,bot (0.40.0-2) [70]
- Scope generalization: integration tests migrate onto init --repo-local (0.40.0-3) [70]
- Scope generalization: cycle close-out, init --scope foundation shipped (0.40.0) [70]

1099	## --scope continuation (0.41.0)
1100	
1101	Continues the foundation laid in 0.40.0 by wiring
1102	`--scope` into the remaining commands.
1103	
1104	**Cycle steps (initial sketch).**
1105	
1106	- **0.41.0-0** — this plan + version bump. Notes only.
1107	- **0.41.0-1** — interlude (unrelated to `--scope`):
1108	  pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for
1109	  `cargo install --path .`. See subsection below.
1110	- **0.41.0-2** — `vc-x1 sync --scope=…`. Sync currently
1111	  hard-codes `[".", ".claude"]` as the default repo set;
1112	  scope drives that instead. Default-scope resolution
1113	  reads `.vc-config.toml > [workspace] other-repo`
1114	  (present + non-empty → `code,bot`, else `code`).
1115	- **0.41.0-3** — `vc-x1 push --scope=…`. Trickier: push's
1116	  state machine assumes dual. Likely splits into a
1117	  single-repo path (skip `commit-claude` + bookmark for
1118	  `.claude` + `finalize-claude`) plus a scope-aware
1119	  preflight summary.
1120	- **0.41.0-4** — `vc-x1 finalize --scope` review.
1121	  Finalize already takes `--repo`; check whether `--scope`
1122	  adds anything or if this step becomes a no-op /
1123	  documentation-only entry.
1124	- **0.41.0 (final)** — cycle close-out + dogfood
1125	  validation on a single-repo project (e.g. apply the
1126	  full sync→edit→push flow against a `--scope=code`
1127	  fixture).
1128	
1129	**Default-scope resolution.** The rule was sketched in
1130	[60] using `app|other|both` vocabulary; restating in the
1131	shipped vocabulary:
1132	
1133	- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=code`.
1134	- `.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo` missing or empty
1135	  → `--scope=code`.
1136	- `.vc-config.toml` with non-empty `other-repo`
1137	  → `--scope=code,bot`.
1138	
1139	**Scope mismatch warnings.** When the resolved scope
1140	disagrees with the working-copy state (e.g. `--scope=code`
1141	in a workspace whose `.vc-config.toml` says dual), emit a
1142	warning so the user can confirm or back out — do not
1143	silently proceed. Fatal vs warn is sub-step decision.
1144	
1145	**Plan is exploratory.** Sub-step boundaries can shift
1146	once each command's call sites are seen. Push in
1147	particular may want its own `-N`-internal state; allow
1148	the cycle to lengthen if needed.
1149	
1150	### 0.41.0-1: pre-commit checklist requires --locked
1151	
1152	Notes-only interlude, unrelated to `--scope`. Hit a
1153	`cargo install --path .` build break on a clean
1154	worktree where `cargo build --release` and
1155	`cargo test --release` both passed.
1156	
1157	Full background — including the upstream cargo issues
1158	and the internals.rust-lang.org design-debate thread —
1159	lives in [notes/cargo-locked-issue.md](cargo-locked-issue.md).
1160	The summary below is enough for the chores log; defer
1161	to that file for the linked references.
1162	
1163	- Multiple `winnow` versions (0.7.x and 1.0.x) in the
1164	  fresh dep graph caused a type mismatch inside
1165	  `gix-object 0.58.0` (its `parse` fn calls
1166	  `gix_actor::signature::decode` whose
1167	  `ErrMode<E>` came from a different `winnow`).
1168	- Root cause: `cargo install` ignores `Cargo.lock`
1169	  by default and re-resolves from scratch, picking
1170	  the newest semver-compatible version of every
1171	  dep independently. The lockfile happens to pin
1172	  one `winnow` that satisfies all gix-* crates; a
1173	  fresh resolve no longer can. `cargo build` /
1174	  `cargo test` use `Cargo.lock` so they're immune.
1175	- Fix: `cargo install --path . --locked`. Cargo has
1176	  no stable config key or env var to make `--locked`
1177	  the default for `cargo install` (verified against
1178	  the cargo config reference), so the discipline
1179	  has to live in the checklist.
1180	
1181	Edits:
1182	
1183	- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-1`.
1184	- `CLAUDE.md`: pre-commit checklist step 4 requires

1189	  `-3`, finalize to `-4`).
1190	- `notes/cargo-locked-issue.md`: new standalone

## --scope continuation (0.41.0)

Continues the foundation laid in 0.40.0 by wiring
`--scope` into the remaining commands.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch).**

- **0.41.0-0** — this plan + version bump. Notes only.
- **0.41.0-1** — interlude (unrelated to `--scope`):
  pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for
  `cargo install --path .`. See subsection below.
- **0.41.0-2** — `vc-x1 sync --scope=…`. Sync currently
  hard-codes `[".", ".claude"]` as the default repo set;
  scope drives that instead. Default-scope resolution
  reads `.vc-config.toml > [workspace] other-repo`
  (present + non-empty → `code,bot`, else `code`).
- **0.41.0-3** — `vc-x1 push --scope=…`. Trickier: push's
  state machine assumes dual. Likely splits into a
  single-repo path (skip `commit-claude` + bookmark for
  `.claude` + `finalize-claude`) plus a scope-aware
  preflight summary.
- **0.41.0-4** — `vc-x1 finalize --scope` review.
  Finalize already takes `--repo`; check whether `--scope`
  adds anything or if this step becomes a no-op /
  documentation-only entry.
- **0.41.0 (final)** — cycle close-out + dogfood
  validation on a single-repo project (e.g. apply the
  full sync→edit→push flow against a `--scope=code`
  fixture).

**Default-scope resolution.** The rule was sketched in
[60] using `app|other|both` vocabulary; restating in the
shipped vocabulary:

- No `.vc-config.toml` → `--scope=code`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo` missing or empty
  → `--scope=code`.
- `.vc-config.toml` with non-empty `other-repo`
  → `--scope=code,bot`.

**Scope mismatch warnings.** When the resolved scope
disagrees with the working-copy state (e.g. `--scope=code`
in a workspace whose `.vc-config.toml` says dual), emit a
warning so the user can confirm or back out — do not
silently proceed. Fatal vs warn is sub-step decision.

**Plan is exploratory.** Sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen. Push in
particular may want its own `-N`-internal state; allow
the cycle to lengthen if needed.

### 0.41.0-1: pre-commit checklist requires --locked

Notes-only interlude, unrelated to `--scope`. Hit a
`cargo install --path .` build break on a clean
worktree where `cargo build --release` and
`cargo test --release` both passed.

Full background — including the upstream cargo issues
and the internals.rust-lang.org design-debate thread —
lives in [notes/cargo-locked-issue.md](cargo-locked-issue.md).
The summary below is enough for the chores log; defer
to that file for the linked references.

- Multiple `winnow` versions (0.7.x and 1.0.x) in the
  fresh dep graph caused a type mismatch inside
  `gix-object 0.58.0` (its `parse` fn calls
  `gix_actor::signature::decode` whose
  `ErrMode<E>` came from a different `winnow`).
- Root cause: `cargo install` ignores `Cargo.lock`
  by default and re-resolves from scratch, picking
  the newest semver-compatible version of every
  dep independently. The lockfile happens to pin
  one `winnow` that satisfies all gix-* crates; a
  fresh resolve no longer can. `cargo build` /
  `cargo test` use `Cargo.lock` so they're immune.
- Fix: `cargo install --path . --locked`. Cargo has
  no stable config key or env var to make `--locked`
  the default for `cargo install` (verified against
  the cargo config reference), so the discipline
  has to live in the checklist.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-1`.
- `CLAUDE.md`: pre-commit checklist step 4 requires
  `--locked`, with a two-line reason inline so a
  future reader doesn't re-litigate.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: this subsection + plan
  renumber (sync moves from `-1` to `-2`, push to
  `-3`, finalize to `-4`).
- `notes/cargo-locked-issue.md`: new standalone
  background doc with the failing build output, the
  asymmetry table, and links to the upstream cargo
  issues + community design discussion.

# References

[57]: /notes/chores-05.md#capture-squash-mode--scope-design-for-push-0374
[60]: #generalize---scope-to-all-commands-design
[61]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-design
[63]: #non-tracking-remote-bookmark-detection-design
[64]: #notes-restructure-chores-06--trim-long-todo-entries-0377
[65]: #scope-design-refinements-0378
[66]: #bookmark-tracking-verification-0380
[67]: #push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-0390
[68]: #source-code-design-ref-convention-design
[69]: #vc-x1-validate-repo-command-design
[70]: #generalize---scope-across-commands-0400
[71]: #--scope-continuation-0410

9	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
10	pub struct InitArgs {
11	    /// Project name (directory and GitHub repo name).
12	    ///
13	    /// - Optional with `--repo-remote` (derived from URL's last
14	    ///   path segment, trailing `.git` stripped).
15	    /// - Required with `--repo-local` and in default mode.
16	    /// - Fatal if both given and they disagree.
17	    #[arg(value_name = "NAME", verbatim_doc_comment)]
18	    pub name: Option<String>,
19	
20	    /// GitHub user or organization.
21	    ///
22	    /// - Only meaningful in default mode.
23	    /// - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.
24	    #[arg(long, verbatim_doc_comment)]
25	    pub owner: Option<String>,
26	
27	    /// Parent directory to create the project under [default: cwd].
28	    ///
29	    /// - Fatal with `--repo-local` (that flag supplies the parent).
30	    #[arg(long, verbatim_doc_comment)]
31	    pub dir: Option<PathBuf>,
32	
33	    /// Create private GitHub repos (default: public).
34	    ///
35	    /// - Only meaningful in default mode.
36	    /// - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.
37	    #[arg(long, verbatim_doc_comment)]
38	    pub private: bool,
39	
40	    /// Dry run — show what would be done without executing
41	    #[arg(long)]
42	    pub dry_run: bool,
43	
44	    /// Max push retries after repo creation [default: 5]
45	    #[arg(long, default_value_t = 5)]
46	    pub push_retries: u32,
47	
48	    /// Seconds between push retries [default: 3]
49	    #[arg(long, default_value_t = 3)]
50	    pub push_retry_delay: u64,
51	
52	    /// Seed repos from template directories.
53	    ///
54	    /// Value is `CODE[,BOT]`. Default bot path is `<CODE>.claude`
55	    /// (file-name concat, not path join — templates are siblings).
56	    ///
57	    /// - With `--scope=code`: only `CODE` is used; passing `,BOT`
58	    ///   is fatal (no session side to seed).
59	    /// - Non-hidden contents copied recursively; hidden entries
60	    ///   (names starting with `.`) are skipped — init writes its
61	    ///   own hidden files.
62	    /// - If a copied tree has a `README.md`, its first line is
63	    ///   rewritten to `# <repo-name>`.
64	    #[arg(long, value_name = "CODE[,BOT]", verbatim_doc_comment)]
65	    pub use_template: Option<String>,
66	
67	    /// Fixture mode — fully self-contained local workspace.
68	    ///
69	    /// Value is a single parent directory.
70	    ///
71	    /// - Project + session at `<PARENT>/<NAME>/` and
72	    ///   `<PARENT>/<NAME>/.claude/`.
73	    /// - Bare origins at `<PARENT>/remote-code.git` and
74	    ///   `<PARENT>/remote-claude.git`.
75	    /// - No `gh`, no network.
76	    /// - Supports `~/…`, `$VAR`, `${VAR}`; relative paths against cwd.
77	    /// - Exclusive with `--repo-remote`, `--dir`, `--owner`, `--private`.
78	    #[arg(long, value_name = "PARENT", verbatim_doc_comment)]
79	    pub repo_local: Option<String>,

83	    /// - Session URL derived: insert `.claude` before `.git` suffix,
84	    ///   or append if no `.git`.
85	    /// - GitHub URLs → `gh repo create` (same as default).
86	    /// - Other URLs → caller pre-creates the remote; init only pushes.
87	    /// - Supports `~/…`, `$VAR`, `${VAR}` in path-shaped values.

106	/// Run a command with retries, sleeping between attempts.
107	fn run_retry(
108	    cmd: &str,
109	    args: &[&str],
110	    cwd: &Path,
111	    retries: u32,
112	    delay_secs: u64,
113	) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
114	    let mut last_err = String::new();
115	    for attempt in 1..=retries {
116	        match run(cmd, args, cwd) {
117	            Ok(out) => {
118	                if attempt > 1 {
119	                    debug!("succeeded after {attempt} attempts");
120	                }

51	## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)
52	
53	Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
54	should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
55	sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
56	right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
57	convention rather than push-only.
58	
59	**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
60	"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
61	be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.
62	
63	**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
64	features): each new command that operates across both repos
65	would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
66	convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
67	existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
68	the same vocabulary.
69	
70	**Rough scope of the change:**
71	
72	- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
73	  `common.rs`).
74	- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
75	  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
76	- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
77	  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
78	- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
79	- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.
80	
81	**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
82	both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
83	deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.
84	
85	## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)
86	
87	Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
88	Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
89	described together because they tackle the same failure mode
90	(silent false success after stale state).
91	
92	**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
93	out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
94	moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
95	finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
96	stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.
97	
98	**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
99	verify saved state matches reality:
100	
101	- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
102	- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
103	- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.
104	
105	On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
106	`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.
107	
108	**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
109	declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
110	bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
111	dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
112	only print when stages genuinely ran or were
113	verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
114	verification.
115	
116	**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
117	then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
118	the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
119	path.
120	
121	## bm-track silent-when-clean (design)
122	
123	Today bm-track prints two lines per command (entry + exit)
124	unconditionally. In steady state both lines say
125	`tracked,tracked` — pure noise. Defer-flagged in 0.37.4 until
126	signal-confidence was established through more dogfood; several
127	0.37.x cycles have now used the probe with no false positives
128	or missed regressions.
129	
130	**Proposed behavior:**
131	
132	- Always probe on entry and exit (no change).
133	- Print on entry only when state isn't fully tracked.
134	- Print on exit when state isn't fully tracked OR when the exit
135	  state differs from entry.
136	- When printing on exit, include the entry state in the output
137	  so the transition is explicit:
138	  `bm-track vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>`.
139	
140	The entry-state-in-exit-output requirement is important —
141	"entry was ok (not printed), exit failed" is inference;
142	"entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one line is evidence.
143	
144	**Implementation note:** record entry state in a local variable
145	at the enter call site and pass into the exit call.
146	
147	## Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)
148	
149	Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
150	**per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
151	Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
152	so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
153	surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
154	(push-app already succeeded in our case).
155	
156	**Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
157	`jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
158	command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.
159	
160	**Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**
161	
162	- `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark
163	  track`). Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion
164	  anyway.
165	- `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
166	  Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
167	  workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
168	  `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
169	  Verify and fix.
170	- `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
171	  establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push.
172	  Works correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked`
173	  on a fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition
174	  sanity check would naturally cover it.
175	- `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
176	- `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
177	- `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
178	  failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.
179	
180	**Shared helper:** `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark,
181	remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
182	`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
183	rather than parsing human-readable output.
184	
185	## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)
186	
187	Notes-only release. Captures six refinements to the scope and
188	squash designs ([57], [60]) from a 2026-04-23 dogfood discussion
189	(the manual squash workflow used to fold late changes into the
190	0.37.7 commit, plus follow-up Q&A on cwd independence and
191	plain-old-repo handling).
192	
193	**Scope semantic invariant.** With `--scope=app|other|both`
194	(per [60]):
195	
196	- `--scope=both` (or omitted; `both` is the default) → dual-repo
197	  operation.
198	- `--scope=app` → single-repo operation on the app repo (direct
199	  reference).
200	- `--scope=other` → single-repo operation on the "other" repo
201	  (indirect reference; resolves via `.vc-config.toml`'s
202	  `[workspace].other-repo` field, which already exists).
203	
204	Anything that isn't `both` is single-repo. The dispatcher branches
205	once on this invariant; new commands inheriting `--scope` get the
206	semantic for free.
207	
208	**`--other` is project config, not a per-command flag.** Came up
209	during the same discussion: should there be a `--other=PATH` flag
210	to override the project's "other" repo per command? Answer: no.
211	That belongs in `.vc-config.toml` (already there as
212	`[workspace].other-repo`). Keeping `--other` out of the
213	per-command surface keeps the flag set small and the per-command
214	vocabulary uniform across all commands — "other" always resolves
215	the same way.
216	
217	**Cwd independence + counterpart-rename suggestion.** Scope names
218	are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative. Each repo has its own
219	`.vc-config.toml` self-describing its role:
220	
221	- App's config: `[workspace] path = "/" other-repo = ".claude"`.
222	- `.claude`'s config: `[workspace] path = "/.claude"
223	  other-repo = ".."`.
224	
225	`path = "/"` identifies the app repo (only the app has it). The
226	dispatcher reads the local config, determines "am I app or other?"
227	via `path`, then resolves `--scope=app` and `--scope=other` to the
228	right physical paths regardless of cwd. From `cd .claude`:
229	
230	- `--scope=app` → `..` (the app repo, found via `.claude`'s
231	  `other-repo`).
232	- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
233	  → I AM the workspace's "other").
234	
235	Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
236	counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
237	the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
238	role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
239	from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
240	`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
241	= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
242	`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.
243	
244	**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
245	be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
246	states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:
247	
248	- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
249	  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
250	  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
251	  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
252	- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
253	  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
254	  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
255	  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
256	  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
257	- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
258	  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
259	  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.
260	
261	Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
262	`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
263	the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
264	identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
265	app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
266	pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
267	state corrupted), not POR fallback.
268	
269	**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
270	the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:
271	
272	- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
273	  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
274	  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
275	- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.
276	- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=other` → squash other only.
277	
278	**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23).** The manual recipe was applied
279	successfully to both repos in sequence to fold late changes into
280	the 0.37.7 commit. Observations worth carrying into the `--squash`
281	implementation:
282	
283	- jj's `jj squash --ignore-immutable` preserves the changeID of
284	  the squashed-into commit. Both repos' ochid trailers stayed
285	  valid through the squash without any fixup. The future
286	  `--squash` implementation can rely on this — no ochid rewrite
287	  needed.
288	- The trimmed "Late changes after push" recipe (0.37.6, two
289	  lines for the `@-` case) worked identically on both sides;
290	  no `jj bookmark set` needed since the bookmark moves with the
291	  rewritten commit.
292	- Push state was already cleared at the end of the prior
293	  successful push (state reported "completed all stages (state
294	  cleared)"). The post-recovery `--restart` / `rm` step
295	  documented in CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback" wasn't
296	  needed in this case — but the doc remains correct for cases
297	  where prior push didn't clean up.
298	
299	- `notes/chores-06.md` — this subsection.
300	- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + new `[65]` reference + new
301	  Todo entry for the template restructure (vc-template-x1 +
302	  `.claude/` subdir, captured separately for later
303	  implementation).
304	
305	## Bookmark tracking verification (0.38.0)
306	
307	Cross-command tracking verification per the [63] design — every
308	repo-modifying command checks its target bookmark for non-tracking
309	remote refs and errors with the exact remediation command if any
310	are found. Multi-step rollout:
311	
312	- **0.38.0-0** — shared helper + tests (foundational refactor; no
313	  behavior change beyond finalize).
314	- **0.38.0-1** — wire into setup commands (`init`, `clone`,
315	  `test-fixture`).
316	- **0.38.0-2** — wire into preflight commands (`sync`, `push`).
317	- **0.38.0** — release commit (notes + any doc tweaks).
318	
319	### 0.38.0-0: shared helper + tests
320	
321	`finalize` already had `find_non_tracking_remote` + a preflight
322	check at finalize.rs:163,215-232 (added in an earlier 0.37.x
323	release). Step 0 promotes that logic into `common.rs` so the
324	remaining commands can share it without copy-paste.
325	
326	Signature deviates slightly from the [63] sketch
327	(`verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)`): kept the existing
328	"detect any non-tracking remote for this bookmark" semantic
329	rather than checking a specific `(bookmark, remote)` pair —
330	matches what the call sites actually want, and avoids a second
331	helper layer.
332	
333	```rust
334	pub fn find_non_tracking_remote(list_output: &str, bookmark: &str) -> Option<String>;
335	pub fn verify_tracking(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
336	```
337	
338	`verify_tracking` runs `jj bookmark list -a <bookmark> -R <repo>`,
339	parses the output via `find_non_tracking_remote`, and returns
340	`Err` with the standard message:
341	
342	```
343	bookmark '{b}' has non-tracking remote '{b}@{r}' —
344	run `jj bookmark track {b} --remote={r} -R {repo}` to fix
345	```
346	
347	- `src/common.rs` — added `find_non_tracking_remote` + `verify_tracking`
348	  + 4 parser tests (moved from `finalize.rs`).
349	- `src/finalize.rs` — removed the local `find_non_tracking_remote`
350	  fn + 4 tests; preflight now calls `crate::common::verify_tracking`.
351	  Behavior unchanged.

## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
convention rather than push-only.

**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.

**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
features): each new command that operates across both repos
would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
the same vocabulary.

**Rough scope of the change:**

- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
  `common.rs`).
- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.

**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.

## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)

Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
described together because they tackle the same failure mode
(silent false success after stale state).

**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.

**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
verify saved state matches reality:

- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.

On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.

**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
only print when stages genuinely ran or were
verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
verification.

**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
path.

## bm-track silent-when-clean (design)

Today bm-track prints two lines per command (entry + exit)
unconditionally. In steady state both lines say
`tracked,tracked` — pure noise. Defer-flagged in 0.37.4 until
signal-confidence was established through more dogfood; several
0.37.x cycles have now used the probe with no false positives
or missed regressions.

**Proposed behavior:**

- Always probe on entry and exit (no change).
- Print on entry only when state isn't fully tracked.
- Print on exit when state isn't fully tracked OR when the exit
  state differs from entry.
- When printing on exit, include the entry state in the output
  so the transition is explicit:
  `bm-track vc-x1 <cmd>: enter=<state> → exit=<state>`.

The entry-state-in-exit-output requirement is important —
"entry was ok (not printed), exit failed" is inference;
"entry=tracked, exit=NOT_TRACKED" in one line is evidence.

**Implementation note:** record entry state in a local variable
at the enter call site and pass into the exit call.

## Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)

Diagnosed 2026-04-22 dogfood: jj's tracking state is
**per-workspace** (local `.jj` store), not shared via git refs.
Sync across machines transfers refs but not the tracking flag —
so a fresh workspace fetched-into never auto-tracks. The failure
surfaces only when `jj git push` is attempted, which is too late
(push-app already succeeded in our case).

**Policy (decided):** error loudly, with the exact
`jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation
command. No self-heal — keeps the fix explicit and visible.

**Scope (every command that creates or mutates repo state):**

- `vc-x1 init`: already correct (Step 10 calls `jj bookmark
  track`). Add the check as a post-condition sanity assertion
  anyway.
- `vc-x1 clone`: does `git clone` then `jj git init --colocate`.
  Whether that combination auto-establishes tracking in jj's
  workspace store is unclear; probably needs an explicit
  `jj bookmark track` for each cloned bookmark after the init.
  Verify and fix.
- `vc-x1 test-fixture`: Step 7's `jj git push --bookmark main`
  establishes tracking as a side effect of the first push.
  Works correctly — confirmed via `jj bookmark list --tracked`
  on a fresh fixture. No change needed; the post-condition
  sanity check would naturally cover it.
- `vc-x1 sync` preflight: detect + error.
- `vc-x1 push` preflight: detect + error (before any mutation).
- `vc-x1 finalize`: detect + error before the squash, so a
  failed push doesn't leave a half-finalized state.

**Shared helper:** `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark,
remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
rather than parsing human-readable output.

## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)

Notes-only release. Captures six refinements to the scope and
squash designs ([57], [60]) from a 2026-04-23 dogfood discussion
(the manual squash workflow used to fold late changes into the
0.37.7 commit, plus follow-up Q&A on cwd independence and
plain-old-repo handling).

**Scope semantic invariant.** With `--scope=app|other|both`
(per [60]):

- `--scope=both` (or omitted; `both` is the default) → dual-repo
  operation.
- `--scope=app` → single-repo operation on the app repo (direct
  reference).
- `--scope=other` → single-repo operation on the "other" repo
  (indirect reference; resolves via `.vc-config.toml`'s
  `[workspace].other-repo` field, which already exists).

Anything that isn't `both` is single-repo. The dispatcher branches
once on this invariant; new commands inheriting `--scope` get the
semantic for free.

**`--other` is project config, not a per-command flag.** Came up
during the same discussion: should there be a `--other=PATH` flag
to override the project's "other" repo per command? Answer: no.
That belongs in `.vc-config.toml` (already there as
`[workspace].other-repo`). Keeping `--other` out of the
per-command surface keeps the flag set small and the per-command
vocabulary uniform across all commands — "other" always resolves
the same way.

**Cwd independence + counterpart-rename suggestion.** Scope names
are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative. Each repo has its own
`.vc-config.toml` self-describing its role:

- App's config: `[workspace] path = "/" other-repo = ".claude"`.
- `.claude`'s config: `[workspace] path = "/.claude"
  other-repo = ".."`.

`path = "/"` identifies the app repo (only the app has it). The
dispatcher reads the local config, determines "am I app or other?"
via `path`, then resolves `--scope=app` and `--scope=other` to the
right physical paths regardless of cwd. From `cd .claude`:

- `--scope=app` → `..` (the app repo, found via `.claude`'s
  `other-repo`).
- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
  → I AM the workspace's "other").

Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.

**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:

- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.

Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
state corrupted), not POR fallback.

**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:

- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=other` → squash other only.

**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23).** The manual recipe was applied
successfully to both repos in sequence to fold late changes into
the 0.37.7 commit. Observations worth carrying into the `--squash`
implementation:

- jj's `jj squash --ignore-immutable` preserves the changeID of
  the squashed-into commit. Both repos' ochid trailers stayed
  valid through the squash without any fixup. The future
  `--squash` implementation can rely on this — no ochid rewrite
  needed.
- The trimmed "Late changes after push" recipe (0.37.6, two
  lines for the `@-` case) worked identically on both sides;
  no `jj bookmark set` needed since the bookmark moves with the
  rewritten commit.
- Push state was already cleared at the end of the prior
  successful push (state reported "completed all stages (state
  cleared)"). The post-recovery `--restart` / `rm` step
  documented in CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback" wasn't
  needed in this case — but the doc remains correct for cases
  where prior push didn't clean up.

- `notes/chores-06.md` — this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + new `[65]` reference + new
  Todo entry for the template restructure (vc-template-x1 +
  `.claude/` subdir, captured separately for later
  implementation).

## Bookmark tracking verification (0.38.0)

Cross-command tracking verification per the [63] design — every
repo-modifying command checks its target bookmark for non-tracking
remote refs and errors with the exact remediation command if any
are found. Multi-step rollout:

- **0.38.0-0** — shared helper + tests (foundational refactor; no
  behavior change beyond finalize).
- **0.38.0-1** — wire into setup commands (`init`, `clone`,
  `test-fixture`).
- **0.38.0-2** — wire into preflight commands (`sync`, `push`).
- **0.38.0** — release commit (notes + any doc tweaks).

### 0.38.0-0: shared helper + tests

`finalize` already had `find_non_tracking_remote` + a preflight
check at finalize.rs:163,215-232 (added in an earlier 0.37.x
release). Step 0 promotes that logic into `common.rs` so the
remaining commands can share it without copy-paste.

Signature deviates slightly from the [63] sketch
(`verify_tracking(repo, bookmark, remote)`): kept the existing
"detect any non-tracking remote for this bookmark" semantic
rather than checking a specific `(bookmark, remote)` pair —
matches what the call sites actually want, and avoids a second
helper layer.

```rust
pub fn find_non_tracking_remote(list_output: &str, bookmark: &str) -> Option<String>;
pub fn verify_tracking(repo: &Path, bookmark: &str) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;
```

`verify_tracking` runs `jj bookmark list -a <bookmark> -R <repo>`,
parses the output via `find_non_tracking_remote`, and returns
`Err` with the standard message:

```
bookmark '{b}' has non-tracking remote '{b}@{r}' —
run `jj bookmark track {b} --remote={r} -R {repo}` to fix
```

- `src/common.rs` — added `find_non_tracking_remote` + `verify_tracking`
  + 4 parser tests (moved from `finalize.rs`).
- `src/finalize.rs` — removed the local `find_non_tracking_remote`
  fn + 4 tests; preflight now calls `crate::common::verify_tracking`.
  Behavior unchanged.
- `notes/chores-06.md` — new `## Bookmark tracking verification
  (0.38.0)` parent + this `### 0.38.0-0` sub-section + stubs for
  `### 0.38.0-1` / `### 0.38.0-2`.

# vc-config: Vibe Coding workspace configuration
#
# workspace-path is this repo's path relative to the workspace root.
# Used to resolve changeID paths in git trailers (e.g. ochid: /changeID).
# other-repo is the relative path to the counterpart repo.

28	/// Banner string emitted as the first line of normal command runs
29	/// and shown at the top of subcommand `--help` output. Built from
30	/// `Cargo.toml`'s name + version at compile time so it stays in
31	/// sync with the bumped version.
32	const BANNER: &str = concat!(env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"), " ", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
33	
34	/// Top-level about line — name, version, and the project tagline
35	/// on a single line. Used as the top-level `about` so `vc-x1 -h`
36	/// reads as one banner-plus-tagline header instead of two stacked
37	/// lines.
38	const TOP_ABOUT: &str = concat!(
39	    env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"),
40	    " ",
41	    env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
42	    " - jj workspace tooling"
43	);

45	/// Build the clap command tree with `BANNER` set as `before_help`
46	/// on every subcommand (transitively). Top-level skips `before_help`
47	/// because its own `about` already carries the name+version+tagline.
48	/// Walks via `mut_subcommand` so individual subcommand
49	/// `#[command(long_about = ...)]` blocks don't have to repeat the
50	/// banner text.
51	fn cli_with_banner() -> clap::Command {
52	    fn add_to_subs(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
53	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
54	            .get_subcommands()
55	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
56	            .collect();
57	        for name in names {
58	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
59	        }
60	        cmd
61	    }
62	    fn add_with_banner(mut cmd: clap::Command) -> clap::Command {
63	        cmd = cmd.before_help(BANNER);
64	        let names: Vec<String> = cmd
65	            .get_subcommands()
66	            .map(|c| c.get_name().to_string())
67	            .collect();
68	        for name in names {
69	            cmd = cmd.mut_subcommand(name, add_with_banner);
70	        }
71	        cmd
72	    }
73	    add_to_subs(Cli::command())
74	}

150	        Default is --check (verify only) — fatal if any repo needs\n\
151	        action. Re-run with --no-check to apply (rebase / fast-forward).\n\
152	        Per repo:\n  \
153	          - up-to-date        nothing to do\n  \
154	          - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote\n  \
155	          - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)\n  \
156	          - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts\n  \

content.text:
I've got the lay of the land. Here's my read of 0.41.0-2 before I start writing code:

**Goal**: replace sync's hard-coded `[".", ".claude"]` with scope-driven repo resolution, mirroring how `init` already exposes `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`.

**Proposed shape**:

1. Add `--scope=SIDES` to `SyncArgs` (clap-parsed `Vec<Side>` like init), `conflicts_with = "repos"` so `--scope` and `-R` are mutually exclusive — they answer different questions ("workspace roles" vs "arbitrary repo set"). `-R` stays for back-compat and multi-repo setups.

2. New helpers — likely in `common.rs` so push/finalize can reuse next step:
   - `default_scope() -> Scope` — walks for workspace root, reads its `.vc-config.toml`'s `[workspace].other-repo`. Non-empty → `Scope(Code,Bot)`, missing/empty → `Scope(Code)`, no workspace root (POR) → `Scope(Code)`.
   - `scope_to_repos(&Scope) -> Vec<PathBuf>` — `Code` → workspace root; `Bot` → `workspace_root.join(other-repo)`. Errors loudly if `Bot` requested but `other-repo` is absent.

3. `sync()` precedence:
   - `-R` non-empty → existing `resolve_repos` path.
   - Else `--scope` set → `scope_to_repos(&Scope(args.scope))`.
   - Else → `scope_to_repos(&default_scope())`.

4. Side-effect benefit: cwd-portable. From `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` (no flags) walks up to find the workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path — current behavior fails there because `[".", ".claude"]` is cwd-relative.

5. Integration tests stay on `sync_repos(&fx.repos(), …)` — bypass the resolver, no breakage. Add unit tests covering the three default-resolution cases (POR, single-repo workspace, dual-repo workspace).

**Open question for you** — anything about scope vocabulary or precedence I should adjust before I start? Otherwise I'll proceed: bump to `0.41.0-2`, implement, run the pre-commit checklist, and stop for review.

message.content: I don't like SIDES I first thought is SCOPE_VAL or SCOPEV or SCOPE or APPBOT or AP thoughts

content.text:
My take, ranked:

1. **`<CODE|BOT|CODE,BOT>`** — best. Shows the literal allowed values and the comma form right in the help. Mirrors the existing `--use-template <CODE[,BOT]>` style. A reader sees `--scope <CODE|BOT|CODE,BOT>` and doesn't need to read a paragraph to know what's accepted.
2. **`<SCOPE>`** — flag-name match (clap's default convention; matches `--bookmark <BOOKMARK>`, `--remote <REMOTE>` already used here). Terser, less self-documenting.
3. `<SIDES>` — current, accurate-but-abstract. The status quo.

Skip:
- `SCOPE_VAL` / `SCOPEV` — meta filler ("the value of --scope") with no extra information.
- `APPBOT` — re-introduces the `app` vocabulary that 0.40.0 deliberately dropped in favor of `code/bot`.
- `AP` — too cryptic; a fresh reader has no chance.

Recommendation: go with `<CODE|BOT|CODE,BOT>` for sync and retroactively update `init` to match. One consistent pattern across the surface, and the help is self-explanatory.

Want me to proceed with that?

It's a one-line change plus a doc tweak, fits naturally inside this commit.

410	
411	```
412	vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, --check (verify)
413	vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
414	vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # dual-repo, apply
415	vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
416	vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
417	```
418	
419	Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
420	explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
421	explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
422	default.
423	
424	| Flag | Description |
425	|------|-------------|
426	| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
427	| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
428	| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
429	| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
430	| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
431	| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |
432	
433	**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:
434	
435	- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
436	  `sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`. Nothing else.
437	  Makes "sprinkle sync everywhere" genuinely cheap. Scope is
438	  bookmark-vs-remote tracking — `@` may have uncommitted
439	  working-copy changes; sync intentionally doesn't speak to
440	  that (use `jj st` for working-copy state).
441	- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
442	  state lines, then **fatal in `--check` mode** with
443	  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
444	  and re-run`. Under `--no-check`, the actions run instead.
445	- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
446	  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.
447	
448	**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
449	tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
450	remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
451	`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
452	configs where auto-advance is disabled.

file.content:
```
vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, --check (verify)
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # dual-repo, apply
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

**Output shape.** Sync collapses output based on what it finds:

- **All up-to-date** — one-line summary:
  `sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`. Nothing else.
  Makes "sprinkle sync everywhere" genuinely cheap. Scope is
  bookmark-vs-remote tracking — `@` may have uncommitted
  working-copy changes; sync intentionally doesn't speak to
  that (use `jj st` for working-copy state).
- **Action needed** (`behind` / `diverged`) — per-repo fetch +
  state lines, then **fatal in `--check` mode** with
  `sync: N repos need action — resolve with vc-x1 sync --no-check
  and re-run`. Under `--no-check`, the actions run instead.
- **`--quiet`** — no output at any level; exit code is the only
  signal. Intended for scripts that just need success/failure.

**Note on the `behind` case.** jj's `git fetch` already fast-forwards a
tracked local bookmark when it's a strict ancestor of the incoming
remote, so in the common case `sync` reports `up-to-date` rather than
`behind`. The `behind` branch covers untracked bookmarks and edge
configs where auto-advance is disabled.

### finalize

Atomically squash, set bookmark, and push a jj repo. The primary use
case is the bot finalizing its own session repo (`.claude`) at the end
of a session. The bot can't just `jj commit` because the act of
committing generates more session data — files written after the commit

2	use std::process::Command;

4	use clap::Args;
5	use log::{LevelFilter, debug, info, warn};

8	use crate::scope::{Scope, Side};
9	
10	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
11	///
12	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
13	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
14	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
15	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
16	///
17	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
18	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
19	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
20	///
21	/// Repo set is resolved (in order):
22	///
23	/// - `-R` / `--repo` → exactly that list (back-compat / arbitrary multi-repo).
24	/// - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` → workspace roles, resolved via the
25	///   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
26	/// - Neither → default scope: `code,bot` if the workspace's
27	///   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
28	///   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
29	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
30	pub struct SyncArgs {
31	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
32	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
33	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
34	    pub check: bool,
35	
36	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
37	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
38	    pub no_check: bool,
39	
40	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
41	    #[arg(short, long)]
42	    pub quiet: bool,
43	
44	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
45	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
46	    pub bookmark: String,
47	
48	    /// Remote to sync against
49	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
50	    pub remote: String,
51	
52	    /// Path to jj repo; repeatable or comma-separated.
53	    ///
54	    /// Mutually exclusive with `--scope`. When neither is given the
55	    /// repo set is derived from `--scope` defaults.
56	    #[arg(
57	        short = 'R',
58	        long = "repo",
59	        value_name = "PATH",
60	        conflicts_with = "scope"
61	    )]
62	    pub repos: Vec<PathBuf>,
63	
64	    /// Which side(s) of the workspace to sync.
65	    ///
66	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`:
67	    ///
68	    /// - `code` — single-repo operation on the app repo.
69	    /// - `bot` — single-repo operation on the bot repo (errors if the
70	    ///   workspace doesn't define one).
71	    /// - `code,bot` — dual-repo (default in a dual-repo workspace).
72	    #[arg(
73	        long,
74	        value_name = "SCOPE",
75	        value_delimiter = ',',
76	        verbatim_doc_comment
77	    )]
78	    pub scope: Option<Vec<Side>>,
79	}
80	
81	/// Split the caller's `-R` flags into a concrete repo list.
82	///
83	/// Each value is split on `,` and trimmed, so `-R .,.claude` works
84	/// identically to `-R . -R .claude`. Caller is responsible for not
85	/// passing an empty slice — `sync()` consults `--scope` instead.
86	fn split_repos(raw: &[PathBuf]) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
87	    raw.iter()
88	        .flat_map(|p| {
89	            p.to_string_lossy()
90	                .split(',')
91	                .map(|s| PathBuf::from(s.trim()))
92	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
93	        })
94	        .collect()
95	}
96	
97	/// Resolve `args` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos` operates on.
98	///
99	/// Precedence: explicit `-R` → `--scope` → workspace-default scope.
100	/// See [`SyncArgs`] for the full contract.
101	fn resolve_args_to_repos(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
102	    if !args.repos.is_empty() {
103	        return Ok(split_repos(&args.repos));
104	    }
105	    let workspace_root = find_workspace_root();
106	    let scope = match &args.scope {
107	        Some(sides) if sides.is_empty() => return Err("--scope: value is empty".into()),
108	        Some(sides) => Scope(sides.clone()),
109	        None => default_scope(workspace_root.as_deref()),
110	    };
111	    scope_to_repos(&scope, workspace_root.as_deref())
112	}
113	
114	/// Relationship between a local bookmark and its remote counterpart.
115	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
116	pub enum State {
117	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
118	    UpToDate,
119	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
120	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
121	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
122	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
123	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
124	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
125	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
126	    NoRemote,
127	}
128	
129	/// Per-repo context accumulated between the snapshot and action phases.
130	#[derive(Debug)]
131	struct RepoCtx {
132	    path: PathBuf,
133	    #[allow(dead_code)]
134	    op_id: String,
135	    state: State,
136	}
137	
138	/// CLI entry point for the `sync` subcommand.
139	///
140	/// Thin wrapper over `sync_repos` that resolves the `-R` flag into a
141	/// concrete repo list (falling back to the dual-repo default) and
142	/// forwards the rest of the args. Tests call `sync_repos` directly
143	/// with absolute fixture paths.
144	///
145	/// When `--quiet` is set, the global log filter is temporarily clamped
146	/// to `Warn` for the duration of the call and restored on return, so
147	/// `info!` calls throughout sync (plus any subprocess-stderr routed
148	/// through `common::run`) go dark. Errors still surface at `Warn` /
149	/// `Error` so script callers don't lose diagnostics.
150	pub fn sync(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
151	    let repos = resolve_args_to_repos(args)?;
152	    if args.quiet {
153	        let prev = log::max_level();
154	        log::set_max_level(LevelFilter::Warn);
155	        let result = sync_repos(&repos, args);
156	        log::set_max_level(prev);
157	        result
158	    } else {
159	        sync_repos(&repos, args)
160	    }
161	}

content.content:
vc-x1 0.41.0-2
---
vc-x1 0.41.0-2

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.

Repo set defaults to the dual-repo workspace pair (`.` and
`.claude`); override with `-R` / `--repo` for single-repo
projects or arbitrary multi-repo workspaces. Flag is repeatable
or comma-separated (e.g. `-R . -R .claude` or `-R .,.claude`).

Default is --check (verify only) — fatal if any repo needs
action. Re-run with --no-check to apply (rebase / fast-forward).
Per repo:
  - up-to-date        nothing to do
  - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote
  - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)
  - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts
  - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip

Scripts and automation should pass --check or --no-check
explicitly — defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the
contract. Interactive use can rely on the default.

After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly
advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing
working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up
orphaned on a stale branch.

On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via
`jj op restore`. Working-copy files are preserved across the
revert — the operation log rewinds but disk content stays.

Output shape:
  - all-up-to-date: one-line summary (`sync: N repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`)
  - action needed (--check):  per-repo fetch + state + fatal error
  - action needed (--no-check): per-repo fetch + state + actions
  - --quiet:        no output; exit code signals success

Usage: vc-x1 sync [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --check
          Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action. This
          is the default; pass explicitly in scripts

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

          - `code,bot` — dual-repo (default in a dual-repo workspace).
          
          [possible values: code, bot]

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

input.old_string:
```
vc-x1 sync                            # dual-repo default, --check (verify)
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # dual-repo, apply
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # single-repo project
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated [default: `.,.claude`] |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |
input.new_string:
```
vc-x1 sync                            # workspace-default scope, --check
vc-x1 sync --check                    # explicit form of the default
vc-x1 sync --no-check                 # workspace-default scope, apply
vc-x1 sync --scope=code               # only the app repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=bot                # only the bot repo
vc-x1 sync --scope=code,bot           # both (explicit form of the dual default)
vc-x1 sync -R .                       # arbitrary repo set (no workspace lookup)
vc-x1 sync -R .,.claude -R ../other   # mixed: repeat + comma-separate
```

Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
explicitly rather than rely on the default — defaults can shift,
explicit flags lock in the contract. Interactive use can take the
default.

**Repo set resolution.** Sync picks the repo list in this order:

1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list.
2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles, resolved via the
   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml` (`code` → root,
   `bot` → `root.join(other-repo)`).
3. Neither — workspace-default scope: `code,bot` if
   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.

`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive — they answer different
questions ("arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles"). Scope is
cwd-portable: from `.claude/`, `vc-x1 sync` walks up to the
workspace root and resolves repos by absolute path.

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-R, --repo <PATH>` | Repo to sync; repeatable or comma-separated. Mutually exclusive with `--scope` |
| `--scope <SCOPE>` | `code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles to sync. Mutually exclusive with `-R` |
| `--check` | Verify only — fetch + classify, error if any repo needs action (default) |
| `--no-check` | Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase/fast-forward as needed |
| `-q, --quiet` | Suppress all output; exit code signals result (for scripts) |
| `--bookmark <NAME>` | Bookmark to sync in each repo [default: main] |
| `--remote <NAME>` | Remote to sync against [default: origin] |

input.new_string:
- `notes/cargo-locked-issue.md`: new standalone
  background doc with the failing build output, the
  asymmetry table, and links to the upstream cargo
  issues + community design discussion.

### 0.41.0-2: sync --scope

Wires `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` into `vc-x1 sync` and
generalizes its repo-resolution rules.

**Repo-set precedence (decided).** Sync now resolves its
operand list in this order:

1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list (back-compat /
   arbitrary multi-repo).
2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles via the
   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
3. Neither — workspace-default scope, per [71]: `code,bot`
   when `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`.
   POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.

`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive (`conflicts_with =
"scope"` on the `repos` field). They answer different
questions — "arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles" — so
combining them is ambiguous, not additive.

**Cwd-portable.** Previously `[".", ".claude"]` was a literal
relative-path default; running `vc-x1 sync` from `.claude/`
tried to resolve `.claude/.claude` and failed. Scope
resolution walks up to the workspace root and emits absolute
paths, so the same command works from anywhere inside the
workspace.

**Helpers in `common.rs`.** Three helpers, designed to be
reused by push (0.41.0-3) and finalize (0.41.0-4):

- `find_workspace_root_from(start)` / `find_workspace_root()`
  — walks up looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
  The cwd-anchored wrapper replaces `main.rs`'s private copy
  (now deleted; `bm_track` calls the shared helper).
- `default_scope(workspace_root)` — applies the [71] rule
  for the no-flag default case.
- `scope_to_repos(scope, workspace_root)` — maps a `Scope`
  to concrete paths. Errors with the [65]-prescribed messages
  when `bot` is requested but no other-repo is configured
  (or no workspace at all).

**Vocabulary alignment.** Renamed `--scope`'s clap
`value_name` from `SIDES` (init's original choice) to `SCOPE`
on both `init` and `sync`. Doc comments now lead with
`SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot` so the help reads as the same
self-describing pattern across commands. Future scope-aware
commands inherit the convention.

**Dogfood (2026-04-26).** Validated all four shapes from app
root and from `.claude/`: default, `--scope=code,bot`,
`--scope=code`, `--scope=bot`. Output count noun pluralizes
correctly (`1 repo` vs `2 repos`). `-R` back-compat preserved.
Conflict + POR error paths exercised against `/tmp` cwd.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-2`.
- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` `value_name` `SIDES` → `SCOPE`,
  doc comment leads with `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `src/common.rs`: `find_workspace_root_from`,
  `find_workspace_root`, `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos`
  + 11 unit tests.
- `src/main.rs`: drop the local `find_workspace_root` copy;
  `bm_track` switches to `common::find_workspace_root`. Sync
  `long_about` rewritten for the new resolution rules.
- `src/sync.rs`: `--scope` flag with
  `conflicts_with = "scope"` on `repos`. New `split_repos` /
  `resolve_args_to_repos` replace the old
  `DEFAULT_REPOS = [".", ".claude"]` literal.
  Three new clap-parse tests + four updated split tests
  (renamed from `resolve_repos_*`); integration `apply_args`
  picks up the new `scope: None` field.
- `README.md`: sync section rewritten — new examples table,

toolUseResult.newString:
- `notes/cargo-locked-issue.md`: new standalone
  background doc with the failing build output, the
  asymmetry table, and links to the upstream cargo
  issues + community design discussion.

### 0.41.0-2: sync --scope

Wires `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` into `vc-x1 sync` and
generalizes its repo-resolution rules.

**Repo-set precedence (decided).** Sync now resolves its
operand list in this order:

1. `-R` / `--repo` — exactly that list (back-compat /
   arbitrary multi-repo).
2. `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` — workspace roles via the
   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
3. Neither — workspace-default scope, per [71]: `code,bot`
   when `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`.
   POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.

`-R` and `--scope` are mutually exclusive (`conflicts_with =
"scope"` on the `repos` field). They answer different
questions — "arbitrary repo set" vs "workspace roles" — so
combining them is ambiguous, not additive.

**Cwd-portable.** Previously `[".", ".claude"]` was a literal
relative-path default; running `vc-x1 sync` from `.claude/`
tried to resolve `.claude/.claude` and failed. Scope
resolution walks up to the workspace root and emits absolute
paths, so the same command works from anywhere inside the
workspace.

**Helpers in `common.rs`.** Three helpers, designed to be
reused by push (0.41.0-3) and finalize (0.41.0-4):

- `find_workspace_root_from(start)` / `find_workspace_root()`
  — walks up looking for `.vc-config.toml` with `path = "/"`.
  The cwd-anchored wrapper replaces `main.rs`'s private copy
  (now deleted; `bm_track` calls the shared helper).
- `default_scope(workspace_root)` — applies the [71] rule
  for the no-flag default case.
- `scope_to_repos(scope, workspace_root)` — maps a `Scope`
  to concrete paths. Errors with the [65]-prescribed messages
  when `bot` is requested but no other-repo is configured
  (or no workspace at all).

**Vocabulary alignment.** Renamed `--scope`'s clap
`value_name` from `SIDES` (init's original choice) to `SCOPE`
on both `init` and `sync`. Doc comments now lead with
`SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot` so the help reads as the same
self-describing pattern across commands. Future scope-aware
commands inherit the convention.

**Dogfood (2026-04-26).** Validated all four shapes from app
root and from `.claude/`: default, `--scope=code,bot`,
`--scope=code`, `--scope=bot`. Output count noun pluralizes
correctly (`1 repo` vs `2 repos`). `-R` back-compat preserved.
Conflict + POR error paths exercised against `/tmp` cwd.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-2`.
- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` `value_name` `SIDES` → `SCOPE`,
  doc comment leads with `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `src/common.rs`: `find_workspace_root_from`,
  `find_workspace_root`, `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos`
  + 11 unit tests.
- `src/main.rs`: drop the local `find_workspace_root` copy;
  `bm_track` switches to `common::find_workspace_root`. Sync
  `long_about` rewritten for the new resolution rules.
- `src/sync.rs`: `--scope` flag with
  `conflicts_with = "scope"` on `repos`. New `split_repos` /
  `resolve_args_to_repos` replace the old
  `DEFAULT_REPOS = [".", ".claude"]` literal.
  Three new clap-parse tests + four updated split tests
  (renamed from `resolve_repos_*`); integration `apply_args`
  picks up the new `scope: None` field.
- `README.md`: sync section rewritten — new examples table,
  resolution-precedence list, `--scope` flag row.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: Done entry + In Progress update.

Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes

Usage: vc-x1 sync [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --check                Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo
                             needs action. This is the default; pass explicitly
                             in scripts
  -v, --verbose...           Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace
      --log <LOG>            Log file path (captures all levels)

  -R, --repo <PATH>          Path to jj repo; repeatable or comma-separated
      --scope <SCOPE>        Which side(s) of the workspace to sync. [possible
                             values: code, bot]
  -h, --help                 Print help (see more with '--help')
