# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0 wires
     scope into sync, push, and finalize.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]
- Push hardening: cycle close-out, 0.39.0-2 skipped (0.39.0) [67]
- Scope generalization: init --repo-local + --repo-remote (0.40.0-1) [70]
- Scope generalization: init --scope=code|bot|code,bot (0.40.0-2) [70]
- Scope generalization: integration tests migrate onto init --repo-local (0.40.0-3) [70]
- Scope generalization: cycle close-out, init --scope foundation shipped (0.40.0) [70]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0-2 wires
     scope into sync; push (0.41.0-3) and finalize
     (0.41.0-4) still to go.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]

content.text:
All checklist items green: `fmt`, `clippy -D warnings`, 254 tests pass, `cargo install --locked` succeeds, retest still green.

Summary of 0.41.0-2:

- **`Cargo.toml`** — bump to `0.41.0-2`.
- **`src/sync.rs`** — new `--scope SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot` flag, `conflicts_with` `-R`. `split_repos` + `resolve_args_to_repos` replace the old `DEFAULT_REPOS = [".", ".claude"]` literal. Three new clap-parse tests + four renamed split tests; integration `apply_args` picks up `scope: None`.
- **`src/init.rs`** — `--scope` `value_name` `SIDES` → `SCOPE`, doc comment leads with `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`. Wording fixed from "side(s)" → "repo(s)" per your call.
- **`src/common.rs`** — three new helpers: `find_workspace_root_from` / `find_workspace_root` (lifted from `main.rs`), `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos`. 11 unit tests covering POR, single-repo workspace, dual workspace, and the bot-without-other-repo error paths.
- **`src/main.rs`** — drops the private `find_workspace_root`; `bm_track` now calls `common::find_workspace_root`. Sync's `long_about` rewritten to describe the new resolution rules.
- **`README.md`** — sync section rewritten with new examples, a precedence list, and the `--scope` flag row.
- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — new `### 0.41.0-2: sync --scope` subsection.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress entry updated; Done entries added for `0.41.0-1` and `0.41.0-2`.

Dogfooded all four scope shapes (default, `code,bot`, `code`, `bot`) from both the app root and `.claude/` cwd — all match expectations, with the `1 repo` / `2 repos` count noun pluralizing correctly. `-R` back-compat preserved; clap rejects `--scope` + `-R` together; POR + `--scope=bot` errors with the documented message.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.text:
It's clap's auto-layout rule, not anything I set deliberately.

clap-derive picks two-column vs over-under per command based on the widest flag spec relative to terminal width. With `max_term_width = 80` (set on the top-level `Cli`), the threshold for the left column is roughly 1/3 of that. When any flag exceeds it, clap switches the whole command to next-line layout for visual consistency.

- **sync** — widest spec is `--bookmark <BOOKMARK>` at ~21 chars. Fits the two-column budget.
- **init** — widest is `--push-retry-delay <PUSH_RETRY_DELAY>` at ~37 chars. Pushes past the threshold and forces over-under for every flag in init.

The offenders are init's `push_retries` / `push_retry_delay` fields — they have no explicit `value_name`, so clap defaults to the upper-cased field name (`<PUSH_RETRIES>`, `<PUSH_RETRY_DELAY>`).

If you want init back to two-column, easiest fix is shorter `value_name`s on those two:

```rust
#[arg(long, default_value_t = 5, value_name = "N")]
pub push_retries: u32,

#[arg(long, default_value_t = 3, value_name = "SECS")]
pub push_retry_delay: u64,
```

That'd drop the longest spec to `--push-retry-delay <SECS>` (~25 chars) and clap should switch back to two-column.

Want me to apply that as part of this commit (or as a separate cleanup), or leave init's help in over-under?

message.content: Can't we chose

content.text:
Yes, clap exposes `Command::next_line_help(true)` to force the over-under layout. The reverse — forcing two-column when the widest spec exceeds the budget — isn't supported; clap will always wrap in that case.

So the choices are:

1. **Uniform over-under everywhere** — set `next_line_help(true)` on the root `Cli` (or apply it via the existing `cli_with_banner` walker that already touches every subcommand). One look across the whole CLI; each flag's description gets full-width room. Costs vertical space when it isn't needed (e.g. `chid -h`, `desc -h`).

2. **Uniform two-column where it fits** — keep the auto-pick, but shrink offending flags (init's retry pair) so every command lands in two-column. Trickier to maintain — every new long flag needs awareness.

3. **Status quo (mixed)** — clap auto-picks per command. Sync stays two-column, init stays over-under.

My take: option 1 if you want the visual consistency you're flagging. The vertical cost is real but minor on a help screen, and it eliminates the surprise of layout shifting per subcommand.

Which do you want?

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0-2 wires
     scope into sync; push (0.41.0-3) and finalize
     (0.41.0-4) still to go.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

message.content: Is there a test where of sync using a --repo-local?

content.text:
Sort of — let me be precise.

**What we have**: every sync integration test uses `Fixture::new(...)` which builds the workspace via `init_with_symlink` in `--repo-local` mode (test_helpers.rs:82). So the fixture *is* a real `--repo-local`-generated dual-repo workspace.

**What we don't have**: those tests call `sync_repos(&fx.repos(), ...)` directly with explicit absolute paths — they bypass `sync()` and the new `resolve_args_to_repos` / `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` chain entirely. None of them exercise the resolver against an init-generated `.vc-config.toml`.

The new helpers' unit tests (`default_scope_dual_workspace`, `scope_to_repos_dual`, etc.) build hand-written `.vc-config.toml` files, not init-generated ones. They prove the helpers work; they don't prove init's output is shaped the way the helpers expect.

That's a real coverage gap. Closing it cleanly: add an integration test that builds a `Fixture` and then calls `default_scope(Some(&fx.work))` and `scope_to_repos(&Scope([Code, Bot]), Some(&fx.work))`, asserting they resolve to `[fx.work, fx.claude]`. That exercises `init --repo-local`'s output against the new resolver end-to-end.

(Going further — driving `sync()` itself instead of `sync_repos()` — would need a cwd change, which isn't safe under parallel `cargo test` without serialization plumbing. Not worth it for this commit.)

Want me to add the integration test before we commit?

message.content: y

10	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
11	///
12	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
13	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
14	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
15	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
16	///
17	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
18	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
19	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
20	///
21	/// Repo set is resolved (in order):
22	///
23	/// - `-R` / `--repo` → exactly that list (back-compat / arbitrary multi-repo).
24	/// - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` → workspace roles, resolved via the
25	///   workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`.
26	/// - Neither → default scope: `code,bot` if the workspace's
27	///   `[workspace] other-repo` is non-empty, else `code`. POR (no
28	///   `.vc-config.toml`) → `code` resolved to cwd.
29	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
30	pub struct SyncArgs {
31	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
32	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
33	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
34	    pub check: bool,
35	
36	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
37	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
38	    pub no_check: bool,
39	
40	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
41	    #[arg(short, long)]
42	    pub quiet: bool,
43	
44	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
45	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
46	    pub bookmark: String,
47	
48	    /// Remote to sync against
49	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
50	    pub remote: String,
51	
52	    /// Path to jj repo; repeatable or comma-separated.
53	    ///
54	    /// Mutually exclusive with `--scope`. When neither is given the
55	    /// repo set is derived from `--scope` defaults.
56	    #[arg(
57	        short = 'R',
58	        long = "repo",
59	        value_name = "PATH",
60	        conflicts_with = "scope"
61	    )]
62	    pub repos: Vec<PathBuf>,
63	
64	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
65	    ///
66	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`:
67	    ///
68	    /// - `code` — single-repo operation on the app repo.
69	    /// - `bot` — single-repo operation on the bot repo (errors if the
70	    ///   workspace doesn't define one).
71	    /// - `code,bot` — dual-repo (default in a dual-repo workspace).
72	    #[arg(
73	        long,
74	        value_name = "SCOPE",
75	        value_delimiter = ',',
76	        verbatim_doc_comment
77	    )]
78	    pub scope: Option<Vec<Side>>,
79	}
80	
81	/// Split the caller's `-R` flags into a concrete repo list.
82	///
83	/// Each value is split on `,` and trimmed, so `-R .,.claude` works
84	/// identically to `-R . -R .claude`. Caller is responsible for not
85	/// passing an empty slice — `sync()` consults `--scope` instead.
86	fn split_repos(raw: &[PathBuf]) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
87	    raw.iter()
88	        .flat_map(|p| {
89	            p.to_string_lossy()
90	                .split(',')
91	                .map(|s| PathBuf::from(s.trim()))
92	                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
93	        })
94	        .collect()
95	}
96	
97	/// Resolve `args` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos` operates on.
98	///
99	/// Precedence: explicit `-R` → `--scope` → workspace-default scope.
100	/// See [`SyncArgs`] for the full contract.
101	fn resolve_args_to_repos(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
102	    if !args.repos.is_empty() {
103	        return Ok(split_repos(&args.repos));
104	    }
105	    let workspace_root = find_workspace_root();
106	    let scope = match &args.scope {
107	        Some(sides) if sides.is_empty() => return Err("--scope: value is empty".into()),
108	        Some(sides) => Scope(sides.clone()),
109	        None => default_scope(workspace_root.as_deref()),
110	    };
111	    scope_to_repos(&scope, workspace_root.as_deref())
112	}

115	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
116	pub enum State {
117	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
118	    UpToDate,
119	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
120	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
121	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
122	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
123	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
124	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
125	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
126	    NoRemote,
127	}
128	
129	/// Per-repo context accumulated between the snapshot and action phases.
130	#[derive(Debug)]
131	struct RepoCtx {
132	    path: PathBuf,

138	/// CLI entry point for the `sync` subcommand.
139	///
140	/// Thin wrapper over `sync_repos` that resolves the `-R` flag into a
141	/// concrete repo list (falling back to the dual-repo default) and
142	/// forwards the rest of the args. Tests call `sync_repos` directly
143	/// with absolute fixture paths.
144	///
145	/// When `--quiet` is set, the global log filter is temporarily clamped
146	/// to `Warn` for the duration of the call and restored on return, so
147	/// `info!` calls throughout sync (plus any subprocess-stderr routed
148	/// through `common::run`) go dark. Errors still surface at `Warn` /
149	/// `Error` so script callers don't lose diagnostics.
150	pub fn sync(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
151	    let repos = resolve_args_to_repos(args)?;
152	    if args.quiet {
153	        let prev = log::max_level();
154	        log::set_max_level(LevelFilter::Warn);
155	        let result = sync_repos(&repos, args);
156	        log::set_max_level(prev);
157	        result
158	    } else {
159	        sync_repos(&repos, args)
160	    }
161	}

Test added: `sync::integration_tests::resolver_chain_against_init_repo_local` builds a real `init --repo-local` fixture, asserts that `find_workspace_root_from(&fx.claude)` walks up to `fx.work`, that `default_scope` returns dual against the init-generated config, and that all three `scope_to_repos` shapes resolve to the right absolute paths. Caps it off by feeding the resolved list to `sync_repos` and asserting up-to-date — proving the resolver's output is shape-compatible with what sync expects.

Test count 254 → 255. fmt/clippy/install all clean.

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes
    #[command(long_about = "Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.

\
        Repo set is resolved (in order):
  \
          - `-R` / `--repo`     exact list (back-compat / arbitrary multi-repo)
  \
          - `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` dual-repo roles via `.vc-config.toml`
  \
          - neither             default scope: `code,bot` when
  \
                                `.vc-config.toml` has a non-empty
  \
                                `[workspace] other-repo`, else `code`
  \
                                (POR resolves to cwd)

\
        Default is --check (verify only) — fatal if any repo needs
\
        action. Re-run with --no-check to apply (rebase / fast-forward).
\
        Per repo:
  \
          - up-to-date        nothing to do
  \
          - behind            fast-forward bookmark to remote
  \
          - ahead             nothing to sync (local has unpushed work)
  \
          - diverged          rebase local onto remote; fail on conflicts
  \
          - no remote         bookmark has no @<remote> counterpart; skip

\
        Scripts and automation should pass --check or --no-check
\
        explicitly — defaults can shift, explicit flags lock in the
\
        contract. Interactive use can rely on the default.

\
        After the bookmark action, `@` is rebased onto the (possibly
\
        advanced) bookmark if it isn't already a descendant, so trailing
\
        working-copy writes (e.g. `.claude`'s `/exit` tail) don't end up
\
        orphaned on a stale branch.

\
        On any failure, every repo is reverted to its starting state via
\

content.text:
Wording change applied to `sync -h`/`--help`:

- **`SyncArgs` struct doc** (sync.rs): "workspace roles … workspace root's `.vc-config.toml`" → "dual-repo roles … project root's `.vc-config.toml`". "POR (no `.vc-config.toml`)" wording unchanged.
- **`--scope` field doc** (sync.rs): "Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync" → "Which repo(s) of the dual-repo to sync"; bullets reworded to "sync only the app repo" / "sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo is configured)" / "sync both repos (the default when both are configured)".
- **`Sync` `long_about`** (main.rs): same swap; the "neither" bullet collapsed to a single line: ``default: `code,bot` when dual, else `code` ``. The literal `[workspace] other-repo` TOML section name remains in the field doc since it's a verbatim file reference.

A couple of related surfaces that *also* contain "workspace" wording but aren't in `sync --help` itself — flagging in case you want them swept too:

1. **README.md** sync section — "workspace roles", "workspace's `[workspace] other-repo`", "workspace root", "workspace-default scope".
2. **`scope_to_repos` error messages** in common.rs (shown when `--scope=bot` is used in a non-dual context): "not in a vc-x1 workspace (no .vc-config.toml with path = \"/\")" — the user sees this at runtime, not in `--help`.
3. **`notes/chores-06.md`** entry I just wrote uses "workspace" liberally.

Want any/all of those swept the same way, or leave them and keep this commit's scope tight to `sync --help`?

message.content: Add a todo consider changing .vc-config.toml to use [repo-list] or some such

input.old_string:
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
input.new_string:
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.

   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0-2 wires
     scope into sync; push (0.41.0-3) and finalize
     (0.41.0-4) still to go.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]
- Push hardening: cycle close-out, 0.39.0-2 skipped (0.39.0) [67]
- Scope generalization: init --repo-local + --repo-remote (0.40.0-1) [70]
- Scope generalization: init --scope=code|bot|code,bot (0.40.0-2) [70]
- Scope generalization: integration tests migrate onto init --repo-local (0.40.0-3) [70]
- Scope generalization: cycle close-out, init --scope foundation shipped (0.40.0) [70]
- Pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for `cargo install` (0.41.0-1) [71]
- Scope continuation: sync --scope (0.41.0-2) [71]

  `find_workspace_root_from`, `default_scope`, and
  `scope_to_repos` against a real `init --repo-local`
  fixture, then drives `sync_repos` on the resolved list
  to confirm shape compatibility.

  resolution-precedence list, `--scope` flag row.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: Done entry + In Progress update.

content.text:
Pre-commit checklist still passes (only doc text changed). Proposed commit:

**Title (~30 chars)**:
```
feat: sync --scope (0.41.0-2)
```

**Body**:
```
Wires --scope=code|bot|code,bot into sync, generalizes its
repo-resolution rules around three new common.rs helpers,
and aligns init's --scope vocabulary while we're in here.

- Cargo.toml: bump to 0.41.0-2.
- src/init.rs: --scope value_name SIDES → SCOPE; doc reworded.
- src/common.rs: find_workspace_root[_from], default_scope,
  scope_to_repos + 11 unit tests.
- src/main.rs: drop the private find_workspace_root; sync
  long_about rewritten for new resolution rules + workspace
  → dual-repo wording sweep.
- src/sync.rs: --scope flag (conflicts_with -R), split_repos
  / resolve_args_to_repos replace the old DEFAULT_REPOS
  literal; three clap-parse tests, four renamed split tests,
  plus a new integration test exercising the resolver chain
  against an init --repo-local fixture; help text picks up
  the same wording sweep.
- README.md: sync section rewritten — examples, precedence
  list, --scope row.
- notes/chores-06.md: 0.41.0-2 subsection.
- notes/todo.md: Done entries (0.41.0-1, 0.41.0-2); two new
  follow-up entries (uniform help layout, [workspace] section
  rename consideration).
```

May I run `vc-x1 push main`?

message.content: Which other subcommands need --scope; push, clone, finalize, chid, desc, list, show, validate-desc, thoughts

content.text:
Quick read per subcommand:

**Already planned in 0.41.0**:
- **push** (0.41.0-3) — needed. Today's state machine assumes dual; single-repo scope means skip `commit-claude`/`finalize-claude`, bookmark one side only.
- **finalize** (0.41.0-4) — *might* be no-op. Already takes `--repo` for a single path. `--scope` would just be a vocabulary alias unless we extend finalize to accept a dual scope and finalize both in sequence (probably not worth it; that's what push does).

**High value, not yet planned**:
- **clone** — yes. Parallel to `init --scope=code|bot|code,bot`. Today clones both URLs; `--scope=code` would clone single-repo from a `vc-template-x1`-shape remote. Symmetric with init; users will expect it.
- **validate-desc / fix-desc** — yes. Both are inherently counterpart-aware (validate-desc reads the *other* repo's commits to check ochid trailers). Scope semantics shift slightly: `--scope=code` means "validate the app repo's commits *against* the bot repo" — which side is the *subject*, not which side is in scope. Useful, but the vocabulary is subtly different from sync/push and may need a renamed flag (e.g. `--side=code|bot`) instead of `--scope`. Worth a design sub-step.

**Low value, vocabulary-only**:
- **chid, desc, list, show** — read-only, multi-repo. Already accept repeatable `-R`/`--repo` (via `CommonArgs`); `--scope=code,bot` would be exactly equivalent to `-R . -R .claude`. Nice for uniformity, ~zero new behavior. Worth deferring until the cycle that touches `CommonArgs`, and doing all four at once.

**No**:
- **symlink** — bot-only by definition (creates `~/.claude/projects/<path>/.claude` symlink). Scope doesn't apply.
- **init, sync** — already done.

Suggested order:
1. **push --scope** (0.41.0-3, already in plan).
2. **finalize --scope** review (0.41.0-4, already in plan; may downgrade to no-op).
3. **clone --scope** — adjacent cycle, mechanically simple.
4. **validate-desc / fix-desc** — separate cycle with its own vocabulary review.
5. **CommonArgs (chid/desc/list/show)** — bottom of the priority list; bundle as one cycle when the value-of-the-vocabulary-uniformity case is felt strongly enough.

Want me to capture #3, #4, #5 as todos so they're not forgotten once the 0.41.0 cycle wraps?

message.content: ATM I go for consistency and --repo would be for backwards compatibility in finalize. As for validate-desc/fix-desc I think scope is fine, code says validate code against bot. bot mean do it it opposite order and code,bot is the default. Same with fix, I see no reason to change vocabulary, IMO

content.text:
Confirming my read of your direction:

- **Consistency principle**: every subcommand that touches one or both repos gets `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`. No vocabulary forks.
- **finalize**: `--scope` becomes the canonical flag; `--repo` stays as a back-compat alias.
- **validate-desc / fix-desc**: keep `--scope` (no rename to `--side`). Semantic: `code` validates code's commits against bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both — that last is the new default.
- **chid/desc/list/show** (via `CommonArgs`): add `--scope` alongside the existing `-R`. Same conflict-or-back-compat call to make as sync.

That gives a sweep of 0.41.0-3 (push), 0.41.0-4 (finalize, now with substance not just review), then post-0.41.0 cycles for clone / validate-desc+fix-desc / CommonArgs.

Want me to capture this as edits to `notes/todo.md` (three new follow-up entries) + a refinement to chores-06.md's [71] plan section noting the finalize back-compat decision and the validate-desc/fix-desc vocabulary call? Single small commit, no functional code change. Or hold the design notes in conversation until 0.41.0-3 starts.

## --scope continuation (0.41.0)

Continues the foundation laid in 0.40.0 by wiring
`--scope` into the remaining commands.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch).**

- **0.41.0-0** — this plan + version bump. Notes only.
- **0.41.0-1** — interlude (unrelated to `--scope`):
  pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for
  `cargo install --path .`. See subsection below.
- **0.41.0-2** — `vc-x1 sync --scope=…`. Sync currently
  hard-codes `[".", ".claude"]` as the default repo set;
  scope drives that instead. Default-scope resolution
  reads `.vc-config.toml > [workspace] other-repo`
  (present + non-empty → `code,bot`, else `code`).
- **0.41.0-3** — `vc-x1 push --scope=…`. Trickier: push's
  state machine assumes dual. Likely splits into a
  single-repo path (skip `commit-claude` + bookmark for
  `.claude` + `finalize-claude`) plus a scope-aware
  preflight summary.
- **0.41.0-4** — `vc-x1 finalize --scope` review.
  Finalize already takes `--repo`; check whether `--scope`
  adds anything or if this step becomes a no-op /
  documentation-only entry.
- **0.41.0 (final)** — cycle close-out + dogfood
  validation on a single-repo project (e.g. apply the
  full sync→edit→push flow against a `--scope=code`
  fixture).

toolUseResult.newString:
In Progress update; two new Todo entries — uniform help
  layout (clap `next_line_help(true)` surfaced when
  comparing `sync -h` two-column vs `init -h` over-under
  during dogfood) and a rename consideration for
  `.vc-config.toml`'s `[workspace]` section
  (`[repo-list]` / `[project]` / `[dual-repo]` candidates;
  breaking change, needs migration).

### 0.41.0-3: capture --scope-everywhere direction

Notes-only step. Records the directional decisions that
came out of the 0.41.0-2 close-out conversation
(2026-04-26) so the rest of the cycle and the post-0.41.0
follow-ups don't relitigate them.

**Consistency principle (decided).** Every subcommand that
operates on one or both repos gets `--scope=code|bot|code,
bot`. No vocabulary forks across commands; `--scope` is the
project-wide convention. Existing per-command flags
(`-R/--repo`, `--repo`) stay as back-compat aliases where
they already exist; new commands inherit `--scope` from day
one.

**Per-command rollout map.**

- `init` — done (0.40.0). `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `sync` — done (0.41.0-2). `--scope` + `-R` (mutually
  exclusive); `-R` retained for arbitrary multi-repo.
- `push` — 0.41.0-4. State machine becomes scope-aware;
  single-side path skips `commit-claude` /
  `finalize-claude` and bookmarks one repo only.
- `finalize` — 0.41.0-5. `--scope` is canonical; existing
  `--repo` becomes a back-compat alias (overrides scope
  when present, prints a soft deprecation note, slated
  for removal in a future cycle). Does *not* downgrade to
  the "review-only / no-op" outcome the original 0.41.0-4
  sketch hedged toward.
- `clone` — post-0.41.0. Parallel to `init --scope`;
  single-repo clone for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — post-0.41.0. Same
  vocabulary as everywhere else, no rename to `--side`.
  Semantic: `--scope=code` validates code's commits
  against bot, `--scope=bot` reverses the direction,
  `--scope=code,bot` does both (the new default).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — post-0.41.0. Add
  `--scope` via `CommonArgs` so all four inherit it
  in one cycle. Existing `-R` / `--repo` stays alongside.
- `symlink` — N/A. Bot-only by definition; scope doesn't
  apply.

**Deferred design questions** (caught in todo, not blocking
the rollout):

- `[workspace]` section name in `.vc-config.toml` reads as
  a Cargo workspace to Rust users but isn't one. Rename
  candidates: `[repo-list]` / `[project]` / `[dual-repo]`.
  Breaking change; needs migration.
- Help layout uniformity — clap auto-picks two-column vs
  over-under per command based on widest flag spec.
  `sync -h` is two-column, `init -h` is over-under. Force
  uniform via `next_line_help(true)` at the root.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-3`.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: refine the [71] cycle-step sketch
  to insert this `-3` notes step and renumber push (was
  `-3`) and finalize (was `-4`); add this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: three new Todo entries — `clone`
  `--scope`, `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`,
  `CommonArgs` (`chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show`) `--scope`.
  In Progress entry updated to reflect the new step
  numbering.

# References

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

1. Continuation of `--scope` rollout. [60],[71]
   - 0.40.0 shipped the `init` foundation; 0.41.0-2 wires
     scope into sync; push (0.41.0-3) and finalize
     (0.41.0-4) still to go.

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

content.content:
179	### Versioning
180	
181	Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based
182	on scope:
183	
184	- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump
185	  directly to `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
186	- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-0`,
187	  implement across multiple commits incrementing the numeric
188	  suffix. The final commit drops the suffix.
189	
190	The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before
191	starting.
192	
193	For multi-step:
194	
195	1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-0` with the plan and commit as a chore
196	   marker.
197	2. Implement in one or more `X.Y.Z-N` commits (increment N as
198	   needed).
199	3. Final commit bumps to `X.Y.Z` (no suffix), updates
200	   `notes/todo.md` and `notes/chores-*.md` — this is the "done"
201	   marker.
202	
203	**Why numeric suffixes (`-0`, `-1`, …) rather than `-devN`:**
204	semver pre-release identifiers may consist of a single numeric
205	component, and they compare numerically per spec. So
206	`X.Y.Z-1 < X.Y.Z-2 < … < X.Y.Z` correctly orders the dev ladder
207	below the done marker. Cargo accepts this form. The `-dev` prefix
208	adds no information the git log doesn't already convey and
209	doubles typing per commit.
210	
211	The final release commit (no suffix) signals completion rather than
212	amending prior commits. This keeps history readable and makes it easy
213	to see which commits were exploratory vs final.
214

file.content:
### Versioning

Every plan must start with a version bump. Choose the approach based
on scope:

- **Single-step** (recommended for mechanical/focused changes): bump
  directly to `X.Y.Z`, implement in one commit. Simpler history.
- **Multi-step** (for exploratory/large changes): bump to `X.Y.Z-0`,
  implement across multiple commits incrementing the numeric
  suffix. The final commit drops the suffix.

The plan should recommend one approach and get user approval before
starting.

For multi-step:

1. Bump version to `X.Y.Z-0` with the plan and commit as a chore
   marker.
2. Implement in one or more `X.Y.Z-N` commits (increment N as
   needed).
3. Final commit bumps to `X.Y.Z` (no suffix), updates
   `notes/todo.md` and `notes/chores-*.md` — this is the "done"
   marker.

**Why numeric suffixes (`-0`, `-1`, …) rather than `-devN`:**
semver pre-release identifiers may consist of a single numeric
component, and they compare numerically per spec. So
`X.Y.Z-1 < X.Y.Z-2 < … < X.Y.Z` correctly orders the dev ladder
below the done marker. Cargo accepts this form. The `-dev` prefix
adds no information the git log doesn't already convey and
doubles typing per commit.

The final release commit (no suffix) signals completion rather than
amending prior commits. This keeps history readable and makes it easy
to see which commits were exploratory vs final.
