file.content:
## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Parallel
   to `init --scope`; supports single-repo clone for
   `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes. Symmetric with init's
   surface so users don't have to relearn it. [60],[71]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`
   flag. Same vocabulary as elsewhere; `code` validates
   code's commits against bot, `bot` reverses the
   direction, `code,bot` does both (new default). [60],[71]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope` to `chid`/`desc`/
   `list`/`show` in one cycle (single shared `CommonArgs`
   change picks all four up). Vocabulary-only — `-R` /
   `--repo` stays as the equivalent today. Low priority;
   bundle when the consistency benefit is felt. [60],[71]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a

content.text: Update the four `--scope` Todo entries to reflect the new vocabulary and the 0.42.0 cycle:

input.old_string:
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Applies
   the generalized convention; warn on scope/WC
   mismatch. [57],[60],[71]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Parallel
   to `init --scope`; supports single-repo clone for
   `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes. Symmetric with init's
   surface so users don't have to relearn it. [60],[71]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`
   flag. Same vocabulary as elsewhere; `code` validates
   code's commits against bot, `bot` reverses the
   direction, `code,bot` does both (new default). [60],[71]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope` to `chid`/`desc`/
   `list`/`show` in one cycle (single shared `CommonArgs`
   change picks all four up). Vocabulary-only — `-R` /
   `--repo` stays as the equivalent today. Low priority;
   bundle when the consistency benefit is felt. [60],[71]
input.new_string:
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Lands in the 0.42.0 cycle alongside the sum-type
   refactor; state machine becomes scope-aware (single-
   side path skips `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/
   `finalize-claude`; `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]

content.text:
All green. Summary of 0.41.0-4 (notes-only):

- **Reverted** the partial push.rs changes (imports + `--scope` flag on `PushArgs`). Push stays unchanged on main; its scope wiring lands in 0.42.0 against the new type model.
- **`Cargo.toml`** — `0.41.0-3` → `0.41.0-4`.
- **`notes/chores-06.md`**:
  - [71] cycle-step sketch updated: `0.41.0-3` marked shipped; `0.41.0-4` redefined as the sum-type capture; old `0.41.0-5` finalize step removed; final close-out reframed to defer push/finalize work to 0.42.0.
  - Legacy default-scope rules marked superseded.
  - New `### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` subsection — settled vocabulary, internal type model, revised default-scope rules, per-command applicability matrix, flag-surface changes (`-s/--scope`, drop `-R`), and rationale for the cycle pivot.
- **`notes/todo.md`**:
  - In Progress dev-ladder updated: `0.41.0-3` done, `0.41.0-4` current; `0.41.0-5` removed; close-out note explains 0.42.0 takes over.
  - Four `--scope` follow-up entries (push, clone, validate-desc/fix-desc, CommonArgs sweep) updated to the new `code|bot|code,bot|<path>` vocabulary and reference the 0.42.0 cycle.

Proposed commit:

**Title**:
```
docs: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary (0.41.0-4)
```

**Body**:
```
Mid-implementation pivot. The original 0.41.0-4 (push --scope on
the existing Vec<Side> shape) surfaced a vocabulary gap — `code`
plays two distinct roles depending on context (a workspace role
vs an implicit cwd fallback in POR). Splitting them honestly is
a type-model change, not a per-command implementation detail —
so the cycle pivots: capture the refined vocabulary, defer all
command implementations to 0.42.0, and close 0.41.0 cleanly.

- Cargo.toml: bump to 0.41.0-4.
- src/push.rs: revert the partial scope work (imports +
  `--scope` flag on PushArgs). Push stays unchanged on main.
- notes/chores-06.md: [71] cycle-steps updated (0.41.0-3
  shipped; 0.41.0-4 redefined; 0.41.0-5 removed); legacy
  default-scope rules marked superseded; new ### 0.41.0-4
  subsection records vocabulary, sum-type model, revised
  defaults, per-command applicability matrix, flag-surface
  changes (-s/--scope, drop -R), and the cycle-pivot
  rationale.
- notes/todo.md: dev-ladder updated for the new shape;
  four --scope follow-up entries (push, clone, validate-desc
  /fix-desc, CommonArgs sweep) updated to the new vocabulary
  and pointed at the 0.42.0 cycle.
```

Work complete. Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit and push.

- `notes/todo.md`: dev-ladder updated (0.41.0-3 → done;
  0.41.0-4 current; 0.41.0-5 removed; 0.41.0 close-out
  noted).

### 0.41.0: cycle close-out

Closes the 0.41.0 cycle. The cycle's original premise —
"wire `--scope` into all dual-repo-aware commands using
the existing `Vec<Side>` shape" — was partially
delivered (sync) and then redirected: 0.41.0-3 captured
the rollout direction, 0.41.0-4 captured the sum-type
vocabulary refinement that supersedes the original
shape. Closing here produces a clean checkpoint before
the bigger refactor.

**What 0.41.0 shipped.**

- `vc-x1 sync --scope=code|bot|code,bot` (0.41.0-2),
  with the supporting helpers `find_workspace_root[_from]`,
  `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos` in `common.rs` plus
  11 unit tests + 1 integration test against an
  `init --repo-local` fixture.
- Pre-commit checklist hardening — `cargo install` must
  pass `--locked` (0.41.0-1), with full background in
  `notes/cargo-locked-issue.md`.
- `--scope`-everywhere direction captured (0.41.0-3):
  consistency principle, per-command rollout map,
  finalize back-compat call, `[workspace]` section
  rename consideration, help-layout uniformity todo.
- `--scope` sum-type vocabulary captured (0.41.0-4):
  `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`,
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` with prefixed-path
  disambiguation, `-s/--scope` short form, `-R` removal,
  per-command applicability matrix, revised default-
  scope rules.

**What 0.41.0 deferred to 0.42.0.**

- The scope sum-type refactor itself (`scope.rs` /
  `common.rs`).
- Custom CLI parser for the keyword-or-path value.
- `init` and `sync` migration onto the new type.
- `push --scope`.
- `finalize --scope` (and `--repo` retirement).
- Any `clone` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` /
  CommonArgs scope wiring — those remain Todo entries
  pointing at the 0.42.0 cycle.

**Dogfood validation.** Each `0.41.0-N` step shipped
through `vc-x1 push main` against the dual-repo
workspace. The cycle did not cover the
`vc-template-x1`-shape single-repo dogfood the original
plan called out — that validation moves to 0.42.0
where the `Single(_)` mode actually exists.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0` (drops the `-N` suffix).
- `notes/chores-06.md`: this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: In Progress cleared (no active cycle);
  Done entries added for `0.41.0-3`, `0.41.0-4`, and
  `0.41.0` close-out.

# References

All green; vc-x1 is now `0.41.0` (no suffix). Summary of the close-out:

- **`Cargo.toml`** — `0.41.0-4` → `0.41.0`.
- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — new `### 0.41.0: cycle close-out` subsection: what shipped (sync --scope, --locked checklist, two design captures), what deferred to 0.42.0 (sum-type refactor + push/finalize/clone/validate-desc/fix-desc/CommonArgs implementations), dogfood honest acknowledgment that `Single(_)` validation moves to 0.42.0.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress cleared (no active cycle, points at 0.42.0 as the next planned cycle); Done entries added for `0.41.0-3`, `0.41.0-4`, and `0.41.0`.

One note before commit: **`CLAUDE2.md` is still in the working tree** (untracked-then-snapshot-committed in the previous push). It's been on `main` since `0.41.0-4`. If you'd rather it not stay parked there until the eventual swap, I can delete it as part of this close-out commit; otherwise it sits as a draft reference in the repo until the post-`--message-file` swap.

Proposed commit:

**Title**:
```
docs: --scope cycle close-out (0.41.0)
```

**Body**:
```
Closes the 0.41.0 cycle. Original premise — wire --scope into
all dual-repo-aware commands using the existing Vec<Side> shape
— delivered partially (sync) before redirecting. 0.41.0-3
captured the rollout direction; 0.41.0-4 captured the sum-type
vocabulary that supersedes the original shape. Closing here
produces a clean checkpoint before the 0.42.0 refactor.

- Cargo.toml: bump to 0.41.0 (drops -N suffix).
- notes/chores-06.md: ### 0.41.0 close-out subsection — what
  shipped (sync --scope; --locked checklist; rollout +
  sum-type design captures), what defers to 0.42.0 (Scope
  sum-type refactor + custom CLI parser + sync/init
  migration + push/finalize/clone/validate-desc/fix-desc/
  CommonArgs scope wiring), and an honest dogfood
  acknowledgment that Single(_) validation moves to 0.42.0
  alongside its implementation.
- notes/todo.md: In Progress cleared (no active cycle); Done
  entries added for 0.41.0-3, 0.41.0-4, and 0.41.0.
```

Want me to delete `CLAUDE2.md` as part of this commit, leave it parked, or hold the close-out and we deal with it separately?

0.41.0 closed and on remote. Three reasonable next moves; want your direction:

1. **0.42.0: `--message-file PATH` cycle.** Smallest, most focused — gets us to the CLAUDE.md swap gate you set earlier. Probably single-step.
2. **0.42.0: scope sum-type refactor.** Multi-step: scope.rs sum type → custom CLI parser → init/sync migration → push --scope → finalize --scope → close. Big but freshest in head.
3. **0.42.0: CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.** You said you wanted this *after* `--message-file` — flagging only as completeness; option 1 has to come first.

I'd lean (1) — small focused cycle, opens the gate to (3), keeps (2) as a clean dedicated cycle later when we have the bandwidth. But your call.

content.text:
You're right. Doing `--message-file` before completing the scope migration leaves us with the worst of both worlds: half the commands on the old `Vec<Side>` shape, half pending the new sum type, and a new push feature added on top of partial state.

Disciplined cycle shape for 0.42.0 — finish what 0.41.0 redirected away from:

- **0.42.0**: scope sum-type refactor + migrate every dual-repo-aware command. Foundation work first, consumers in dependency order.
  - `-0` plan + version bump
  - `-1` `scope.rs` sum type (`Roles | Single`) — internal only
  - `-2` custom CLI parser + retrofit `init` (smallest consumer; proves the parser end-to-end)
  - `-3` retrofit `sync` (drop `-R`, add `-s/--scope`, accept `<path>`)
  - `-4` `push --scope`
  - `-5` `finalize --scope` (replace `--repo`)
  - `-6` `clone --scope`
  - `-7` Single(_) dogfood — apply the full sync→edit→push flow against a POR / single-repo fixture
  - `-N` final close-out

`validate-desc` / `fix-desc` and the CommonArgs sweep (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) stay deferred to 0.43.0 — they're read-side, separate concern from the dual-repo-aware mutation flow. `--message-file PATH` lands after 0.42.0 closes (still gates the CLAUDE.md swap, just on the post-0.42.0 timeline).

Want me to start with 0.42.0-0 (plan + version bump in `Cargo.toml` + new `notes/chores-07.md` since chores-06 is at 1500+ lines and a new cycle is a natural file boundary)?

# Chores-06.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..05.md;
06 starts here only because 05 has gotten long.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7)

Notes-only release. Three doc-hygiene moves bundled:

- Start `notes/chores-06.md` (this file). chores-05.md grew to
  ~1000 lines; new chore entries land here going forward. No
  reservation for design-only — chores-06 is general-purpose.
- Trim long todo entries to 2-3 line summaries; move detail to
  the relevant chores-05 design subsection or a new chores-06
  subsection. One item per top-level list entry, no `##`
  subsections, loose topical ordering only — and order
  highest-priority-first.
- Adopt lazy numbering for Todo items: every entry uses `1. `;
  the markdown renderer auto-numbers. Lets the user reference
  items by displayed number without manual renumbering on
  reorder/insert. Done section stays on `-` bullets — items
  aren't referenced by number once completed.

Design subsections added below for items that previously lived
inline in `notes/todo.md`:

- "Generalize --scope to all commands (design)"
- "Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)"
- "bm-track silent-when-clean (design)"
- "Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection (design)"

Also: removed the redundant "Allow `vc-x1 push` to work on code
or bot repo together or independantly..." entry from todo —
fully subsumed by the `--scope` + `--squash` work.

- `notes/chores-06.md` — new file with intro + this chore entry +
  four design subsections.
- `notes/todo.md` — Todo section rewritten in priority order;
  long entries trimmed to short summaries; redundant entry
  removed; switched to `1. ` lazy numbering; Done entry +
  new `[60]`–`[64]` references added.
- `notes/README.md` — `## Todo format` updated with the
  lazy-numbering convention note.

input.content:
# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [72] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [72]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [71]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [72]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [72] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

# References

[71]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
[72]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-sum-type-vocabulary

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is iteratively rolling out the `--scope` flag across the vc-x1 dual-repo CLI tool. Initial cycle (0.41.0) goal: "getting scope to work 'everywhere'". Mid-cycle, the user redirected to a more disciplined design after surfacing a vocabulary gap. Final 0.41.0 cycle deliveries:
   - 0.41.0-2: sync --scope=code|bot|code,bot (shipped)
   - 0.41.0-3: notes-only — capture --scope-everywhere direction (consistency principle, finalize back-compat, validate-desc/fix-desc vocabulary, CommonArgs sweep)
   - 0.41.0-4: notes-only — capture --scope sum-type vocabulary (Scope enum with Roles/Single, drop -R, add -s, drop "single" keyword in favor of `--scope=<path>` with prefix disambiguation)
   - 0.41.0: cycle close-out, defers all command implementations to 0.42.0
   
   User then directed starting 0.42.0 cycle for the full sum-type refactor + migration of all dual-repo-aware commands. Currently working on 0.42.0-0 (plan + version bump + new chores-07.md).
   
   Most recent user question: "but could we start references at [1] with a new file?" — questioning whether chores-07.md should restart reference numbering or continue from chores-06.md's [71]/[72].

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rust workspace tool (vc-x1) wrapping `jj` (Jujutsu) + git
   - Dual-repo workspace pattern: app repo + `.claude` session repo
   - `.vc-config.toml` workspace metadata (`[workspace] path = "/"`, `other-repo = ".claude"`)
   - `ochid:` trailers for cross-repo commit linking
   - clap-derive CLI parsing with `verbatim_doc_comment`
   - jj operation log + `jj op restore` for atomic rollback
   - vc-x1 push state machine (8 stages: Preflight, Review, Message, CommitApp, CommitClaude, BookmarkBoth, PushApp, FinalizeClaude)
   - Dev ladder with -N suffixes (semver pre-release identifiers)
   - Scope sum type: `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`
   - Three workspace states: POR (no .vc-config.toml), single-repo workspace, dual workspace

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/Cargo.toml**
     - Version progression: 0.41.0-1 → 0.41.0-2 → 0.41.0-3 → 0.41.0-4 → 0.41.0 → 0.42.0-0
     - Currently at 0.42.0-0
   
   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/scope.rs**
     - Defines `Scope(pub Vec<Side>)` and `enum Side { Code, Bot }`
     - Will be refactored in 0.42.0-1 to: `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`
   
   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/common.rs**
     - Added in 0.41.0-2: `find_workspace_root_from`, `find_workspace_root`, `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos` + 11 unit tests
     - These will need updating in 0.42.0 for the sum-type model
   
   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/sync.rs**
     - Got `--scope` flag in 0.41.0-2 (Vec<Side> form)
     - Has `-R/--repo` flag (mutually exclusive with --scope) — will be dropped in 0.42.0-3
     - Doc lists `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot`, "Which repo(s) of the dual-repo to sync."
   
   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/init.rs**
     - Has `--scope` flag (Vec<Side> form), value_name=SCOPE
     - Will migrate in 0.42.0-2
   
   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/src/push.rs**
     - 2267 lines, 8-stage state machine
     - Key functions: `push_in`, `run_from`, `run_stage`, stage_preflight/review/message/commit_app/commit_claude/bookmark_both/push_app/finalize_claude
     - PushState with persistent fields: stage, bookmark, started_at, app_chid, claude_chid, claude_had_changes, op_app, op_claude, title, body
     - Test helper: `test_args(title, body)` creates PushArgs with from=Stage::Message, no_finalize=true, yes=true

     - Will get scope wiring in 0.42.0-4

       - `### 0.41.0-2: sync --scope`

     - [71] cycle-step sketch updated multiple times to reflect cycle pivots

   - **/home/wink/data/prgs/rust/vc-x1/notes/chores-07.md** (NEW FILE created in current turn)

     - Contains `## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)` with cycle plan
     - Sub-steps -0 through -7 + close-out
     - References at bottom: `[71]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410` and `[72]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-sum-type-vocabulary`
     - **User questioning whether to restart references at [1] for a new file**

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **fmt --check false-positive**: Used `cargo fmt --check 2>&1 | tail -3 && echo "fmt OK"` — pipe made next command run even when fmt failed. Fixed by running fmt standalone with `; echo "exit=$?"` to verify.
   - **Help layout wrap issue**: My multi-line `long_about` for sync had continuation lines that wrapped to col 2 instead of aligning under "default scope:". Fixed by collapsing to a single shorter line: `default: code,bot when dual, else code`.
   - **CLAUDE2.md inadvertently committed**: Got snapshotted into 0.41.0-4 push because it was untracked in the working tree. User said "delete it" → removed in 0.41.0 close-out commit.
   - **Mid-implementation pivot on 0.41.0-4**: Had started implementing push --scope (added imports, --scope flag to PushArgs, bumped Cargo.toml to 0.41.0-4) when user said "I think we need to add --scope=single". Reverted partial code, kept Cargo.toml bump, redirected -4 to notes-only design capture.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Vocabulary gap discovery**: User surfaced that `code` plays two distinct roles depending on context (workspace role vs implicit cwd fallback in POR). Resolved by introducing sum type with explicit `Single(PathBuf)` variant.
   - **Disambiguation rule**: For `--scope=<path>`, paths must be prefixed (./, /, ~/) to distinguish from keyword set. Bare names always interpreted as keywords.
   - **Cycle pivot**: Rather than fight through partial 0.41.0-4 implementation on soon-to-be-deprecated Vec<Side> shape, captured design and deferred to 0.42.0.
   - **Disciplined cycle order for 0.42.0**: Foundation first (sum type, parser), smallest consumer (init), then sync, push, finalize, clone, then dogfood. User confirmed by saying "Yes" to start.

6. All user messages:

As for validate-desc/fix-desc I think scope is fine, code says validate code against bot. bot mean do it it opposite order and code,bot is the default. Same with fix, I see no reason to change vocabulary, IMO"
   - "Capture now while we're thinking about it and I'm ready to close -2 if you think we're done"

   - "First I'd like to have you slow down... I'm suggesting. --scope=code|bot|code,bot is always dual-repo and .vc-config.toml must always exist..."
   - "- I don't agree with 'drop the single-repo-workspace concept entirely' - there have been times (making errors) I want to work only one of the repos at a time and want to be explicit and ignore .vc-config.toml - -R,--repo drop multi-repo"
   - "I agree (b) but maybe --scope=<path> and no --repo? (ignore backwards compatibility for the moment)"

   - "2. drop -R eventually use -s,--scope 3. error if 'single' in appropriate 4. close out 0.41.0"
   - "y" (approve revert + design capture)

8. Current Work:
   Working on 0.42.0-0 (notes-only step: plan + version bump + new chores file). Just before the user's question, I had:

file.content:
# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [72] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [72]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [71]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [72]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [72] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

# References

[71]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
[72]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-sum-type-vocabulary

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

(no active cycle — 0.41.0 closed; next cycle is the
0.42.0 sum-type refactor, kicked off when ready)

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Lands in the 0.42.0 cycle alongside the sum-type
   refactor; state machine becomes scope-aware (single-
   side path skips `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/
   `finalize-claude`; `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]

lastPrompt: but could we start references at [1] with a new file?

content.text:
The tradeoff: restarting at `[1]` keeps chores-07 self-contained and easier to read in isolation, but `todo.md`'s reference list is a single global namespace spanning all chores files (today: `[4]` → chores-01, `[71]` → chores-06, etc.) — if chores-07 also has its own `[1]`, citations from `todo.md` become ambiguous unless we either (a) prefix with the file (e.g. `[07-1]`) or (b) keep two parallel namespaces (file-local for cross-references inside a chores file, global in todo.md). Continuing from `[73]` preserves the current single-namespace convention with zero collisions.

My recommendation: continue from `[73]`+ unless you want to take the broader convention change (file-local refs everywhere, with file-prefixed cites in todo.md). The latter is cleaner long-term but is a sweep across all existing references, not just chores-07.

message.content: But the reference from "in progress", "todo" and "done" to references at the bottom of todo.md are all local so we can reuse them and like now we should retire a most/all of the "done" to done.md so we can reuse them, correct?

content.text:
Yes — you're right, I overstated the global-namespace framing. Each file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) has its own local refs; `[71]` in `todo.md` and `[71]` in `chores-07.md` happen to point at the same URL but that's coincidence, not constraint. So:

- `chores-07.md` can start at `[1]` cleanly — the cross-file pointers to chores-06 just become `[1]`/`[2]` in chores-07's local namespace.
- Retiring most of `todo.md`'s Done section to `done.md` frees up those numbers for reuse in todo.md's In Progress / Todo / Done.

Want me to fold both into 0.42.0-0?
- Renumber chores-07 refs to `[1]`/`[2]`
- Retire `todo.md` Done entries (which cutoff — everything pre-0.40, or pre-0.41?) to `done.md`, prune the now-orphaned refs from todo.md's References section

Or split the Done retirement into its own `-N` step before the implementation work starts.

message.content: Yes, and we can do this when closing a ladder or opening a new ladder, agreed

content.text:
Agreed — natural rhythm: at ladder close-out we know what's safely retireable, at ladder open we want fresh numbers. Codifying the convention belongs in `notes/README.md` (it's about notes mechanics) rather than `CLAUDE.md`.

For 0.42.0-0, what's the retirement cutoff for `todo.md` → `done.md`? Options:
- **All Done entries** — clean slate; todo.md's Done section starts empty for the 0.42.0 cycle
- **Everything pre-0.41.0** — keeps the most recent cycle visible in todo.md as recent context
- **Everything pre-0.40.0** — keeps 0.40 + 0.41 visible (both are scope-related, useful adjacent context for 0.42)

I'd lean to the second (pre-0.41.0) — keeps the immediately-preceding cycle visible in todo.md as the "what just shipped" view, which is useful when scanning. But it's your call.

message.content: sg, everyting pre-0.41.0

32	  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
33	- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,

35	  stuck detection.
36	- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step

43	references.
44	
45	Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the

47	renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's

50

52	In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [72] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [72]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [71]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [72]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [72] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

# References

[71]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
[72]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-sum-type-vocabulary

# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [72]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [72] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

- 0.42.0-0 plan + version bump + new chores-07.md (current) [72]
- 0.42.0-1 scope.rs sum type — `Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)` [72]
- 0.42.0-2 custom CLI parser + retrofit `init` --scope [72]
- 0.42.0-3 retrofit `sync` — drop -R, add -s [72]
- 0.42.0-4 push --scope (state-machine becomes scope-aware) [72]
- 0.42.0-5 finalize --scope (replaces --repo) [72]
- 0.42.0-6 clone --scope [72]
- 0.42.0-7 Single(_) dogfood validation [72]
- 0.42.0 close-out [72]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Lands in the 0.42.0 cycle alongside the sum-type
   refactor; state machine becomes scope-aware (single-
   side path skips `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/
   `finalize-claude`; `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- Pre-commit checklist requires `--locked` for `cargo install` (0.41.0-1) [71]

- Scope continuation: capture --scope-everywhere direction (0.41.0-3) [71]
- Scope continuation: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary (0.41.0-4) [71]
- Scope continuation: cycle close-out — push/finalize work deferred to 0.42.0 (0.41.0) [71]

input.new_string:
# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Multiple references must be separated: `[2],[3]` not `[2,3]` or `[2][3]`.
In markdown, `[2,3]` is a single ref key (won't resolve) and `[2][3]`
is parsed as display text `2` with ref key `3` (so `[2]` won't resolve).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [1]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [2],[3]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

+different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.
+`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped

+- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before

+- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

- 0.42.0-0 plan + version bump + new chores-07.md (current) [72]
- 0.42.0-1 scope.rs sum type — `Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)` [72]

- 0.42.0-7 Single(_) dogfood validation [72]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Lands in the 0.42.0 cycle alongside the sum-type
   refactor; state machine becomes scope-aware (single-
   side path skips `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/
   `finalize-claude`; `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

1	# Chores-07.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
5	07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
6	0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
7	boundary.
8	
9	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
10	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
11	Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
12	implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.
13	
14	## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)
15	
16	Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
17	foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
18	--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
19	type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
20	flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
21	path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
22	new shape in dependency order.
23	
24	**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
25	once each command's call sites are seen).**
26	
27	- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
28	  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
29	- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
30	  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
31	  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
32	  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
33	  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
34	  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
35	  follow.
36	- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
37	  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
38	  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
39	  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
40	  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
41	  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
42	  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
43	  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
44	  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
45	- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
46	  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
47	  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
48	  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
49	  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
50	  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
51	  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
52	  exercise the resolver chain.
53	- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
54	  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
55	  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
56	  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
57	  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
58	  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
59	  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
60	  integration tests for each scope shape.
61	- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
62	  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
63	  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
64	  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
65	  intentional break, not a deprecation.
66	- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
67	  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
68	  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
69	- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
70	  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
71	  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
72	  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
73	  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
74	- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
75	  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
76	  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.
77	
78	**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**
79	
80	- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
81	  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
82	  two repos by definition).
83	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
84	  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
85	  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
86	- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
87	  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.
88	
89	**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
90	`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
91	the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
92	applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
93	diving into any of the `-N` steps below.
94

# Chores-07.md

General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
boundary.

Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.

## --scope sum-type refactor (0.42.0)

Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
--scope sum-type vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
new shape in dependency order.

**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
once each command's call sites are seen).**

- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` sum type. Internal only — no
  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
  follow.
- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
  exercise the resolver chain.
- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
  integration tests for each scope shape.
- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
  intentional break, not a deprecation.
- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.

**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**

- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
  two repos by definition).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.

**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope sum-type vocabulary` for
the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
diving into any of the `-N` steps below.

# References

[1]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-sum-type-vocabulary

8:use crate::scope::{Scope, Side};
79:    pub scope: Option<Vec<Side>>,
109:        Some(sides) => Scope(sides.clone()),

lines: //! Which side(s) of a workspace a command should operate on.
-//! - `Scope` wraps the parsed list; a missing flag (None at the arg
-//!   layer) means "caller decides", typically via workspace-state
-//!   (0.40.0)` for the full rationale and the vocabulary history.
+//!     `.vc-config.toml` and operates on the one repo at the
+//!     given path. Wired up incrementally (parser lands in
+//!   arm only; on `Single(_)` they all return `false` /
+//!   distinguish modes match on the enum directly.
+/// One side of a dual-repo workspace.
/// - `Bot` — the Claude Code session repo (typically at `.claude/`).
-/// Parsed `--scope` value: the set of sides the command applies to.
+/// - `Roles(_)` — dual-repo workspace; the requested side set.
+/// - `Single(_)` — explicit single-repo mode at the given path.
-    /// True when the scope includes the code side.
+    /// True when the `Roles` arm includes the code side.
-    /// True when the scope includes the bot side.
+    /// True when the `Roles` arm includes the bot side.
-    /// Exactly the code side — single-repo operation.
+    /// Roles-only with exactly the code side — single-side dual-repo op.
-    /// Exactly the bot side — single-repo operation on the bot.
+    /// Roles-only with exactly the bot side — single-side dual-repo op.
+    /// Roles arm including both sides — full dual-repo op.
-    /// Neither side — empty scope, always an invalid input.
+    /// Roles arm with an empty side list — always invalid input.
+    /// `Single(_)` returns `false` (it carries a path, not a side list).
+        // `Single(_)` is a distinct mode; the Roles-shaped helpers
+        // all report false / not-empty so callers that match on
+        // helpers naturally fall through to mode-aware logic.

### 0.42.0-1: scope.rs sum type

Internal-only refactor: tuple-struct `Scope(Vec<Side>)` →
`enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`. No CLI
surface changes, no consumer logic changes — every existing

is staged for the parser (-2) and the sync resolver (-3).
